\chapter{Getting Started}
\label{\detokenize{getting-started:getting-started}}\label{\detokenize{getting-started::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Welcome to JDXI\sphinxhyphen{}Editor! This section will guide you through everything you need to know to get up and running with your Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer and the JDXI\sphinxhyphen{}Editor software.


\chapter{Getting Started Guide}
\label{\detokenize{getting-started:getting-started-guide}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Introduction}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Learn about JDXI\sphinxhyphen{}Editor and its capabilities

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Installation}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Complete setup guide for all platforms

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Quick Start Guide}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Step\sphinxhyphen{}by\sphinxhyphen{}step tutorial to get you started

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Editor Windows}
\label{\detokenize{editor-windows:editor-windows}}\label{\detokenize{editor-windows::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Editor Windows are the sophisticated command centers of JDXI\sphinxhyphen{}Editor—comprehensive, specialized interfaces that provide deep, granular control over every aspect of your Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.


\chapter{Available Editors}
\label{\detokenize{editor-windows:available-editors}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{JDXi Instrument Window}}} \sphinxhyphen{} The main instrument interface and control center

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Editor Windows Overview}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Comprehensive guide to all specialized editors

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Digital Synth Editors (Parts 1 \& 2)}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Multi\sphinxhyphen{}partial digital synthesis control

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Analog Synth Editor}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Classic analog synthesis with warm, organic sound

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Drum Kit Editor}}} \sphinxhyphen{} Professional drum sound design with 36 individual voices

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Additional Editors}
\label{\detokenize{additional-editors:additional-editors}}\label{\detokenize{additional-editors::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Advanced editing tools and specialized interfaces for professional sound design and performance.


\chapter{Available Editors}
\label{\detokenize{additional-editors:available-editors}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Arpeggiator Editor}}} — Sophisticated pattern generation and sequencing

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Effects Editor}}} — Master effects processing with reverb, delay, compressor, phaser

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Vocal Effects Editor}}} — Vocoder and Auto\sphinxhyphen{}Pitch (OFF/VOCODER/AUTO\sphinxhyphen{}PITCH)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Pattern Sequencer}}} — 4×16 step grid, MIDI load/save

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{MIDI File Player}}} — Track classification, channel assignment, playback

\end{itemize}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxstylestrong{Program Editor Tabs}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The Program Editor includes a \sphinxstylestrong{System Settings} tab for Master Tune, Master Level, Key Shift, RX/TX Program Change/Bank Select, Keyboard Velocity, and Velocity Curve.

\end{description}

\sphinxstepscope


\chapter{Playing MIDI Files}
\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files:playing-midi-files}}\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files::doc}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
JDXI\sphinxhyphen{}Editor provides two main tools for working with MIDI files: the \sphinxstylestrong{Pattern Sequencer} for step\sphinxhyphen{}based editing and the \sphinxstylestrong{MIDI File Player} for playback and track management.


\chapter{Overview}
\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files:overview}}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxstylestrong{MIDI File Player}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load standard MIDI files (.mid, .midi), classify tracks (Detect Drums, Classify Tracks), assign channels to the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi’s four parts (Digital 1, Digital 2, Analog, Drums), and play through the synthesizer or FluidSynth (SoundFont) when no hardware is connected.

\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxstylestrong{Pattern Sequencer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load MIDI files into a 4×16 step grid, edit notes/velocity/duration per step, and save patterns back to MIDI files. Integrates with the MIDI File Player via “Load into Pattern Editor.”

\end{description}


\chapter{Typical Workflow}
\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files:typical-workflow}}\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Load a MIDI file} in the MIDI File Player (File \(\rightarrow\) Open… or drag\sphinxhyphen{}and\sphinxhyphen{}drop)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Classify tracks} (optional): Use Detect Drums and Classify Tracks to auto\sphinxhyphen{}assign channels

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Apply changes}: Apply All Track Changes or Apply Presets

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Play}: Start playback (JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi or FluidSynth)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Edit in Pattern Editor} (optional): Load into Pattern Editor \(\rightarrow\) edit steps \(\rightarrow\) Save Pattern

\end{enumerate}


\chapter{SoundFont Playback}
\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files:soundfont-playback}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
When no JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi is connected, enable \sphinxstylestrong{Enable FluidSynth for local playback} in MIDI Configuration. The MIDI File Player will use a configured SoundFont (.sf2/.sf3) for software playback. See \DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{features\sphinxhyphen{}and\sphinxhyphen{}usage}}} for SoundFont setup.


\chapter{Available Tools}
\label{\detokenize{playing-midi-files:available-tools}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{Pattern Sequencer}}} — 4×16 step grid, MIDI load/save, part muting

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\DUrole{xref}{\DUrole{std}{\DUrole{std-doc}{MIDI File Player}}} — Track classification, channel assignment, playback

\end{itemize}

\sphinxstepscope

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.channel package}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.channel:module-jdxi_editor.midi.channel.channel}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.channel:jdxi-editor-midi-channel-channel-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines an enumeration for MIDI channels used in a synthesizer, specifically for a Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi\sphinxhyphen{}style instrument editor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{MidiChannel} class extends \sphinxtitleref{IntEnum} and provides symbolic names for the common MIDI channels, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} DIGITAL1 (channel 1)
\sphinxhyphen{} DIGITAL2 (channel 2)
\sphinxhyphen{} ANALOG (channel 3)
\sphinxhyphen{} DRUM (channel 10)
\sphinxhyphen{} PROGRAM (channel 16)

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class also provides utility methods for handling MIDI channels, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{\_\_str\_\_}: Returns a string representation of the channel.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{midi\_channel\_number}: A property that returns the actual MIDI channel number (1\sphinxhyphen{}based).
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{from\_midi\_channel}: A class method that retrieves a \sphinxtitleref{MidiChannel} from a given MIDI channel number.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
channel = MidiChannel.DIGITAL1
print(str(channel))  \# Output: “Digital 1 (Ch.1)”
print(channel.midi\_channel\_number)  \# Output: 1
print(MidiChannel.from\_midi\_channel(9))  \# Output: MidiChannel.DRUM\_KIT

\end{description}
\index{MidiChannel (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel)@\spxentry{MidiChannel}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.channel.channel}}

\subparagraph{Example Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:example-usage}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{AddressMemoryAreaMSB}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{Address}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }   \PYG{n}{PROGRAM} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x18}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }   \PYG{n}{TEMPORARY\PYGZus{}TONE} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x19}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Add an offset to a base address}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{addr\PYGZus{}bytes} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{AddressMemoryAreaMSB}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PROGRAM}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{add\PYGZus{}offset}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{addr\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} (0x18, 0x00, 0x20, 0x00)}
\PYG{g+go}{(24, 0, 32, 0)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Get SysEx\PYGZhy{}ready address}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{sysex\PYGZus{}address} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{AddressMemoryAreaMSB}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{PROGRAM}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}sysex\PYGZus{}address}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x20}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{sysex\PYGZus{}address}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{hex}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} \PYGZsq{}18002000\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+go}{18002000}

Base class for Roland\sphinxhyphen{}style hierarchical memory address enums (e.g., 0x18, 0x19, etc.)
Includes lookup, offset arithmetic, and SysEx\sphinxhyphen{}ready address formatting.

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the full 4\sphinxhyphen{}byte address by adding a 3\sphinxhyphen{}byte offset to the base address.
The base address is assumed to be a single byte (e.g., 0x18).
:param address\_offset: Union{[}int, Tuple{[}int, int, int{]}{]} The address offset
:return: tuple{[}int, Any{]} The full 4\sphinxhyphen{}byte address

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.address\_map module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.address.address_map}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:jdxi-editor-midi-data-address-address-map-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.address\_map@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.address\_map}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.address\_map@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.address\_map}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{5. Parameter Address Map}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:parameter-address-map}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} from picomidi.core.parameter.factory import AddressFactory
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} system\_common = AddressFactory.from\_str(“01 00 00 00”)

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{PARAMETER\PYGZus{}ADDRESS\PYGZus{}MAP}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{ParameterAreas}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{SYSTEM}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{ByteGroupKind}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{ADDRESS\PYGZus{}4}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{n}{system\PYGZus{}common}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{AddressStartMSB.SETUP: 0x02}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}**}Transmission of “\#” marked address is divided to some packets. For
example, ABH in hexadecimal notation will be divided to 0AH and
0BH, and is sent/received in this order.
{\color{red}\bfseries{}**}“\textless{}*\textgreater{}” marked address

\sphinxAtStartPar
For reference:
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi (ModelID = 00H 00H 00H 0EH)
+——————————————————————————+
| Start       |                                                                |
| Address     |               Description                                      |
{\color{red}\bfseries{}|\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}+\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}|}
| 01 00 00 00 | Setup                                                          |
{\color{red}\bfseries{}|\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}+\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}|}
| 02 00 00 00 | System                                                         |
{\color{red}\bfseries{}|\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}+\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}|}
| 18 00 00 00 | Temporary Program                                              |
| 19 00 00 00 | Temporary Tone (Digital Synth Part 1)                          |
| 19 20 00 00 | Temporary Tone (Digital Synth Part 2)                          |
| 19 40 00 00 | Temporary Tone (Analog Synth Part)                             |
| 19 60 00 00 | Temporary Tone (Drums Part)                                    |
+——————————————————————————+
{\color{red}\bfseries{}**}System
+——————————————————————————+
| Offset      |                                                                |
| Offset      |                                                                |

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.address.helpers}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:jdxi-editor-midi-data-address-helpers-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
helpers.py
Helper functions for SysEx address manipulation and parsing.
This module provides utilities to apply address offsets, convert addresses to hex strings,
and parse SysEx addresses into a JSON\sphinxhyphen{}like structure.
It also includes functions to find matching symbols in provided base classes.
Functions:
\sphinxhyphen{} apply\_address\_offset: Applies an offset to a base SysEx address.
\sphinxhyphen{} address\_to\_hex\_string: Converts a SysEx address to a hex string.
\sphinxhyphen{} parse\_sysex\_address\_json: Parses a SysEx address into a JSON\sphinxhyphen{}like structure.
\sphinxhyphen{} find\_matching\_symbol: Finds a matching symbol in provided base classes.
\index{apply\_address\_offset() (in module jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers)@\spxentry{apply\_address\_offset()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.helpers}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi\_addresses module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi_addresses}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:jdxi-editor-midi-data-address-jdxi-addresses-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi\_addresses@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi\_addresses}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi\_addresses@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.jdxi\_addresses}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
sysex utils:
\# Get full address for Temporary Drum Kit (Offset from TEMP\_TONE\_BASE)
base = JDXiMemoryAddress.TEMP\_TONE\_BASE
drum\_offset = (0x10, 0x00, 0x00)
drum\_address = base.offset(drum\_offset)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Build a DT1 message to write data to it
data = {[}0x01, 0x02, 0x03{]}
sysex\_msg = JDxiSysExBuilder.build\_dt1(drum\_address, data)

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.parser module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.address.parser}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.address:jdxi-editor-midi-data-address-parser-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.parser@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.parser}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.parser@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.address.parser}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parser
Example Usage
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{class ProgramAddress(Address):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
COMMON = 0x18
DIGITAL\_1 = 0x19

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
sysex\_addr = b’\textquotesingle{}
parsed = parse\_sysex\_address(sysex\_addr, ProgramAddress)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{if parsed:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
base, offset = parsed
print(f”Base: \{base\}, Offset: \{offset\}”)

\sphinxlineitem{else:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
print(“Unknown base address”)

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Output:

\sphinxAtStartPar
from
Base: \textless{}ProgramAddress.COMMON: 0x18\textgreater{}, Offset: (0, 32, 0)

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio package}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.arpeggio:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.arpeggio:jdxi-editor-midi-data-arpeggio-arpeggio-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.arpeggio.arpeggio}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arpeggiator Configuration Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the settings, data structures, and parameter ranges for an arpeggiator.
It provides enumerations, default values, and data classes to represent various arpeggiator
configurations, including grid timing, note duration, motif patterns, and styles.

\sphinxAtStartPar
\#\#\# Contents:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxstylestrong{Arpeggio Settings}:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{arp\_grid}: Available grid timing values (e.g., 1/4, 1/8, etc.).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{arp\_duration}: Note duration options as percentages.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{arp\_motif}: Various motif patterns for the arpeggiator.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{arp\_style}: A collection of predefined arpeggiator styles.

\end{itemize}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Arpeggio Parameter Ranges and Defaults}:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{Arpeggio}: Defines valid parameter ranges and default values.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioPatch}: A dataclass representing a complete arpeggio configuration.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Enumerations}:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioGrid}: Grid timing options.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioDuration}: Possible note durations.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioMotif}: Arpeggiator motif patterns.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioParameters}: Parameter identifiers used in arpeggiator control.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\#\#\# Usage:
This module can be used to configure an arpeggiator in a MIDI editor or synthesizer
application. The \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioPatch} class allows structured representation and validation of
arpeggiator settings, ensuring proper configuration.

DigitalControlChange

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get Cutoff CC value for Partial 2
cutoff\_partial\_2 = DigitalControlChange.get\_cc\_value(“Cutoff”, 2)
print(f”Cutoff (Partial 2): \{cutoff\_partial\_2\}”)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get Envelope NRPN value for Partial 3
envelope\_partial\_3 = DigitalControlChange.get\_nrpn\_value(“Envelope”, 3)
print(f”Envelope (Partial 3): \{envelope\_partial\_3\}”)

\sphinxAtStartPar
envelope\_map = DigitalControlChange.get\_nrpn\_map(“Envelope”)

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-digital-common-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module: AddressParameterDigitalCommon

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the AddressParameterDigitalCommon class, which represents common parameters
for Digital/SuperNATURAL synth tones. These parameters are shared across all partials and
define various synthesizer settings, such as tone name, tone level, performance parameters,
partial switches, and additional effects.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class provides methods to:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieve a human\sphinxhyphen{}readable digital name for each parameter.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Identify if a parameter is a switch (binary or enum).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get appropriate digital text for switch values.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate and convert parameter values within their defined range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieve the partial number (1\sphinxhyphen{}3) for partial\sphinxhyphen{}specific parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get a parameter by its name.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameters include:
\sphinxhyphen{} Tone name parameters (12 ASCII characters)
\sphinxhyphen{} Tone level
\sphinxhyphen{} Performance parameters (e.g., Portamento switch, Mono switch)
\sphinxhyphen{} Partial switches (e.g., Partial 1 switch, Partial 2 switch)
\sphinxhyphen{} Additional effect parameters (e.g., Ring Mod, Unison, Analog Feel)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Initialize a parameter object
param = AddressParameterDigitalCommon(address=0x00, min\_val=0, max\_val=127)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get the digital name for the parameter
print(param.display\_name)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate and convert a value for the parameter
valid\_value = param.validate\_value(64)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get the switch text for a given value
switch\_text = param.get\_switch\_text(1)

\end{description}

\index{RING\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{RING\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam.RING_SWITCH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{RING\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(31, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}Turns ring modulator on/off. \textbackslash{}nBy multiplying partial 1’s OSC and partial 2’s OSC, this creates a complex, metallic\sphinxhyphen{}sounding waveform like that of a bell. \textbackslash{}nIf Ring Switch is turned on, the OSC Pulse Width Mod Depth, OSC Pulse Width, and SUPER SAW\textbackslash{}nDetune of partial 1 and partial 2 cannot be used.\textbackslash{}nIn addition, if an asymmetrical square wave is selected as the OSC waveform, the OSC variation\textbackslash{}nwill be ignored, and there will be a slight difference in sound compared to the originally selected\textbackslash{}n waveform (OFF, ON)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Ring Switch\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{UNISON\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{UNISON\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam.UNISON_SWITCH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{UNISON\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(46, 0, 1, 0, 21, \textquotesingle{}This layers a single sound.\textbackslash{}nIf the Unison Switch is on, the number of notes layered on one key will change according to the\textbackslash{}nnumber of keys you play.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Unison Switch\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PORTAMENTO\_MODE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{PORTAMENTO\_MODE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam.PORTAMENTO_MODE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PORTAMENTO\_MODE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(49, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}NORMAL: Portamento will always be applied.\textbackslash{}nLEGATO: Portamento will be applied only when you play legato (i.e., when you press the next\textbackslash{}nkey before releasing the previous key).\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Portamento Mode\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{LEGATO\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{LEGATO\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{LEGATO\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(50, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the time taken for the pitch to change when playing portamento. Higher values\textbackslash{}nlengthen the time over which the pitch will change to the next note.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Legato Switch\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ANALOG\_FEEL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{ANALOG\_FEEL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam.ANALOG_FEEL}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ANALOG\_FEEL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(52, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Use this to apply “1/f fluctuation,” a type of randomness or instability that is present in many\textbackslash{}nnatural systems (such as a babbling brook or whispering breeze) and is perceived as pleasant by \textbackslash{}nmany people.\textbackslash{}nBy applying “1/f fluctuation” you can create the natural\sphinxhyphen{}sounding instability that is\textbackslash{}ncharacteristic of an analog synthesizer.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Analog Feel\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WAVE\_SHAPE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute)@\spxentry{WAVE\_SHAPE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.common.DigitalCommonParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WAVE\_SHAPE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(53, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Partial 1 will be modulated by the pitch of partial 2. Higher values produce a greater effect.\textbackslash{}nThis has no effect if the partial 1 waveform is PW\sphinxhyphen{}SQR or SP\sphinxhyphen{}SAW.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Wave Shape\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-digital-modify-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{AddressParameterDigitalModify: JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Digital Synthesizer Parameter Mapping}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:addressparameterdigitalmodify-jd-xi-digital-synthesizer-parameter-mapping}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the AddressParameterDigitalModify class for modifying parameters of
Digital/SuperNATURAL synth tones in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides attributes and methods to manage various modulation
parameters shared across all partials of a digital synth tone. It also
includes methods for retrieving digital text representations of switch
values, parameter lookup by name, and value validation.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Create a AddressParameterDigitalModify instance for Attack Time Interval Sensitivity
attack\_time\_param = AddressParameterDigitalModify({\color{red}\bfseries{}*}AddressParameterDigitalModify.ATTACK\_TIME\_INTERVAL\_SENS)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate a value
validated\_value = attack\_time\_param.validate\_value(100)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get digital text for a switch value
text = attack\_time\_param.get\_switch\_text(1)  \# For ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_MODE, returns “FREE\sphinxhyphen{}RUN”

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Retrieve parameter by name
param = AddressParameterDigitalModify.get\_by\_name(“ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_MODE”)
if param:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
print(param.name, param.min\_val, param.max\_val)
\end{quote}

\index{ATTACK\_TIME\_INTERVAL\_SENS (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute)@\spxentry{ATTACK\_TIME\_INTERVAL\_SENS}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam.ATTACK_TIME_INTERVAL_SENS}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ATTACK\_TIME\_INTERVAL\_SENS}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(1, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Shortens the FILTER and AMP Attack Time according to the spacing between note\sphinxhyphen{}on events.\textbackslash{}nHigher values produce a greater effect. With a setting of 0, there will be no effect.\textbackslash{}nThis is effective when you want to play rapid notes using a sound that has a slow attack\textbackslash{}n(Attack Time).\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Attack Time Interval Sens\textquotesingle{})}}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{RELEASE\_TIME\_INTERVAL\_SENS}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(2, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Shortens the FILTER and AMP Release Time if the interval between one note\sphinxhyphen{}on and the next\textbackslash{}nnote\sphinxhyphen{}off is brief. Higher values produce a greater effect. With a setting of 0, there will be no effect.\textbackslash{}nThis is effective when you want to play staccato notes using a sound that has a slow release\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Release Time Interval Sens\textquotesingle{})}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_MODE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute)@\spxentry{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_MODE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam.ENVELOPE_LOOP_MODE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_MODE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(4, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}Use this to loop the envelope between certain regions during a note\sphinxhyphen{}on.\textbackslash{}nOFF The envelope will operate normally.\textbackslash{}nFREE\sphinxhyphen{}RUN When the Decay segment has ended, the envelope will return to the Attack. The Attack through\textbackslash{}nDecay segments will repeat until note\sphinxhyphen{}off occurs.\textbackslash{}nTEMPO\sphinxhyphen{}SYNC Specifies the loop rate as a note value (Sync Note parameter).\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Envelope Loop Mode\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_SYNC\_NOTE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute)@\spxentry{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_SYNC\_NOTE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.modify.DigitalModifyParam.ENVELOPE_LOOP_SYNC_NOTE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ENVELOPE\_LOOP\_SYNC\_NOTE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(5, 0, 19, 0, 19, \textquotesingle{}Returns to the Attack at the specified rate. If the Attack+Decay time is shorter than the specified\textbackslash{}nloop, the sound is maintained at the Sustain Level. If the Attack+Decay time is longer than the\textbackslash{}nspecified loop, the sound returns to the Attack even if the Decay has not completed. This will\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}ncontinue repeating until note\sphinxhyphen{}off occurs.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Envelope Loop Sync Note\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-digital-partial-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DigitalPartialParameter: JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Digital Synthesizer Parameter Mapping}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:digitalpartialparameter-jd-xi-digital-synthesizer-parameter-mapping}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class defines digital synthesizer parameters for the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi, mapping
various synthesis parameters to their corresponding memory addresses and valid
value ranges.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The parameters include:
\sphinxhyphen{} Oscillator settings (waveform, pitch, detune, envelope, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} Filter settings (cutoff, resonance, envelope, key follow, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} Amplitude settings (level, velocity, envelope, pan, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} LFO (Low\sphinxhyphen{}Frequency Oscillator) settings (waveform, rate, depth, sync, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} Modulation LFO settings (waveform, rate, depth, sync, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} Additional synthesis controls (aftertouch, wave gain, super saw detune, etc.)
\sphinxhyphen{} PCM wave settings (wave number, gain, high\sphinxhyphen{}pass filter cutoff, etc.)
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Each parameter is stored as address tuple containing:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(memory\_address, min\_value, max\_value)

\end{description}


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~OSC\_WAVE}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the oscillator waveform preset\_type.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~FILTER\_CUTOFF}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Controls the filter cutoff frequency.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~AMP\_LEVEL}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sets the overall amplitude level.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~LFO\_RATE}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Adjusts the rate of the low\sphinxhyphen{}frequency oscillator.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~MOD\_LFO\_PITCH\_DEPTH}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Modulates pitch using the secondary LFO.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Type}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{Other parameters follow address similar structure.}}

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\_\_init\_\_(self,~address}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
int, min\_val: int, max\_val: int):
Initializes address DigitalParameter instance with an address and value range.

\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage Example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
filter\_cutoff = DigitalParameter(0x0C, 0, 127)  \# Filter Cutoff Frequency
print(filter\_cutoff.address)  \# Output: 0x0C

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class helps structure and manage parameter mappings for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi SysEx processing.
\index{DigitalPartialParam (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial)@\spxentry{DigitalPartialParam}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial.DigitalPartialParam}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.digital.partial.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DigitalPartialParam}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{value}}}
{}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum package}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-drum-package}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum::doc}}

\subparagraph{Submodules}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:submodules}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-drum-addresses-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.addresses}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines mappings for drum kit parameter addresses in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Mappings:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
DRUM\_ADDRESS\_MAP (dict): Maps drum partial names (e.g., “BD1”, “SD1”) to their corresponding parameter addresses.
DRUM\_GROUP\_MAP (dict): Maps MIDI key numbers to their respective drum kit parameter addresses.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
These mappings are used to identify the memory locations of drum parameters when processing MIDI SysEx messages.


\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-drum-common-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.common}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumCommonParameter} class, which represents
common parameters for drum tones in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
These parameters are shared across all partials within a drum kit
and include settings such as tone name, kit level, and various switches.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{DrumCommonParameter(SynthParameter)}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Represents common drum parameters and provides methods
for retrieving addresses, validating values, and formatting
switch\sphinxhyphen{}based parameter values.

\end{description}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-drum-helper-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a utility function for retrieving the parameter address
corresponding to a given partial name within the drum section of the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The function maps partial names to their respective addresses using
\sphinxtitleref{DRUM\_ADDRESS\_MAP}, ensuring correct parameter access for drum sound editing.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_address\_for\_partial\_name(partial\_name: str) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} int}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the parameter address for the specified drum partial name.

\end{description}

\end{description}
\index{get\_address\_for\_partial\_name() (in module jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper)@\spxentry{get\_address\_for\_partial\_name()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.helper}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-drum-partial-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module for defining drum kit parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi editor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the \sphinxtitleref{DrumPartialParameter} class, which extends \sphinxtitleref{SynthParameter}
to define the addresses, value ranges, and characteristics of drum partial parameters.
It includes various attributes representing different drum\sphinxhyphen{}related parameters, such as
tuning, level, panning, and effects settings.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{DrumPartialParameter \textendash{} Represents a drum partial parameter with its address,}
\sphinxAtStartPar
value range, and optional digital range.

\end{description}

\end{description}


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DRUM\_GROUP\_MAP~\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}~Mapping~of~drum~groups.}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{DRUM\_ADDRESS\_MAP~\sphinxhyphen{}\sphinxhyphen{}~Mapping~of~parameter~names~to~MIDI~addresses.}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Example usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
drum\_param = DrumPartialParameter(0x0F, 0, 127)
print(drum\_param.address)  \# Output: 0x0F
print(drum\_param.min\_val)  \# Output: 0
print(drum\_param.max\_val)  \# Output: 127

\end{description}

\index{ASSIGN\_TYPE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{ASSIGN\_TYPE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.ASSIGN_TYPE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ASSIGN\_TYPE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(12, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}Assign Type sets the way sounds are played when the same key is pressed a number of times.\textbackslash{}nMULTI: Layer the sound of the same keys. Even with continuous sounds where the sound plays\textbackslash{}nfor an extended time, such as with crash cymbals, the sounds are layered, without previously\textbackslash{}nplayed sounds being eliminated.\textbackslash{}nSINGLE: Only one sound can be played at a time when the same key is pressed. With continuous\textbackslash{}nsounds where the sound plays for an extended time, the previous sound is stopped when the\textbackslash{}nfollowing sound is played.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{MUTE\_GROUP (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{MUTE\_GROUP}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.MUTE_GROUP}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{MUTE\_GROUP}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(13, 0, 31, 0, 31, \textquotesingle{}On an actual acoustic drum set, an open hi\sphinxhyphen{}hat and a closed hi\sphinxhyphen{}hat sound can never occur\textbackslash{}nsimultaneously.\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}nTo reproduce the reality of this situation, you can set up a Mute Group.\textbackslash{}nThe Mute Group function allows you to designate two or more drum partials that are not allowed\textbackslash{}nto sound simultaneously. Up to 31 Mute Groups can be used.\textbackslash{}nDrum partials that does not belong to any such group should be set to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_LEVEL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_LEVEL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_LEVEL}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_LEVEL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(14, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the volume of the waveform.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe volume of each drum partial is specified by TVA Level (p. 24), and the overall volume of\textbackslash{}nthe entire drum kit is specified by COMMON Kit Level (p. 20).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_COARSE_TUNE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(15, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in semitone steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{} 4 octaves).\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall coarse tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Coarse Tune (p. 22).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_FINE_TUNE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(16, 14, 114, \sphinxhyphen{}50, 50, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in 1\sphinxhyphen{}cent steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{}50 cents).\textbackslash{}n* One cent is 1/100th of a semitone.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall fine tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Fine Tune (p. 22 of maanual).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(17, 0, 30, 0, 30, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the width of random pitch deviation that will occur each time a key is pressed. If you do\textbackslash{}nnot want the pitch to change randomly, set this to “0.” These values are in units of cents (1/100th\textbackslash{}n of a semitone).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_PAN (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_PAN}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_PAN}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_PAN}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(18, 0, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}64, 63, \textquotesingle{}Sets the pan for the drum partial. “L64” is far left, “0” is center, and “63R” is far right.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nUse the Wave Pan parameter (p. 21 f the Manual) to specify the panning of the waveforms that make up\textbackslash{}nthe drum partial.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_RANDOM_PAN_DEPTH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(19, 0, 63, 0, 63, \textquotesingle{}When a loop waveform is selected, the sound will normally continue as long as the key is\textbackslash{}npressed. If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to\textbackslash{}nSUSTAIN.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_ALTERNATE_PAN_DEPTH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(20, 1, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}63, 63, \textquotesingle{}Use this parameter when you want the stereo location to change randomly each time you press\textbackslash{}na key.\textbackslash{}nHigher settings will produce a greater amount of change.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nThis applies only to waves whose Wave Random Pan Sw parameter (p. 21) is ON.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_ENV\_MODE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_ENV\_MODE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to“SUSTAIN.” NO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS, SUSTAIN\textquotesingle{}, 127, None, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(32, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}WMT Velocity Control determines whether a different drum partial is played (ON) or not (OFF)\textbackslash{}ndepending on the force with which the key is played (velocity).\textbackslash{}nWhen set to “RANDOM,” the drum kit’s constituent drum partials will sound randomly,\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}nregardless of any Velocity messages.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to“SUSTAIN.” NO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS, SUSTAIN\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(50, 0, 16, 0, 16, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the depth of the modulation produced by FXM.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nIf Wave Tempo Sync is “ON,” pitch\sphinxhyphen{}related settings (p. 22) and FMX\sphinxhyphen{}related settings have no\textbackslash{}neffect.\textbackslash{}nValues: 0 \sphinxhyphen{} 16\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(51, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes the pitch of the waveform to be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “ON,” the pitch of the waveform will be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “OFF,” the pitch of the waveform will not be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_COARSE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(52, 16, 112, \sphinxhyphen{}48, 48, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in semitone steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{} 4 octaves).\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall coarse tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Coarse Tune (p. 22).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(54, 0, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}64, 63, \textquotesingle{}Sets the pan for the drum partial. “L64” is far left, “0” is center, and “63R” is far right.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nUse the Wave Pan parameter (p. 21 f the Manual) to specify the panning of the waveforms that make up\textbackslash{}nthe drum partial.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(55, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}Use this setting to cause the waveform’s panning to change randomly each time a key is pressed\textbackslash{}n(ON) or not (OFF).\textbackslash{}n* To specify the range in which pan is changed, edit Random Pan Depth (p. 24).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_WAVE_ALTERNATE_PAN_SWITCH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(56, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes panning of the waveform to be alternated between left and right each time a\textbackslash{}nkey is pressed.\textbackslash{}nSet Alter Pan Sw to “ON” to pan the Wave according to the Alter Pan Depth settings, or to\textbackslash{}n“REVS” when you want the panning reversed.\textbackslash{}nIf you do not want the panning to change each time a key is pressed, set this to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(57, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the volume of the waveform.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe volume of each drum partial is specified by TVA Level (p. 24), and the overall volume of\textbackslash{}nthe entire drum kit is specified by COMMON Kit Level (p. 20).\textbackslash{}nValues: 0 \sphinxhyphen{} 127\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

Make these settings when you want\textbackslash{}ndifferent waveforms to sound in response to notes played at different strengths.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nIf you attempt to set the lower key velocity above the higher, or the higher key velocity\textbackslash{}nbelow the lower, the other setting changes in tandem.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

Make these settings when you want\textbackslash{}ndifferent waveforms to sound in response to notes played at different strengths.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

If you\textbackslash{}nwant notes played outside the specified key velocity range to not be sounded at all, set this to 0\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT4\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(266, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes the pitch of the waveform to be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “ON,” the pitch of the waveform will be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “OFF,” the pitch of the waveform will not be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT4\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(271, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes panning of the waveform to be alternated between left and right each time a\textbackslash{}nkey is pressed.\textbackslash{}nSet Alter Pan Sw to “ON” to pan the Wave according to the Alter Pan Depth settings, or to\textbackslash{}n“REVS” when you want the panning reversed.\textbackslash{}nIf you do not want the panning to change each time a key is pressed, set this to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{PITCH\_ENV\_LEVEL\_4 (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PITCH\_ENV\_LEVEL\_4}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.PITCH_ENV_LEVEL_4}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PITCH\_ENV\_LEVEL\_4}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(289, 1, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}63, 63, \textquotesingle{}Specify the pitch envelope levels (Level 0\textendash{}Level 4).\textbackslash{}nThese specify the amount of pitch change at each point relative to the standard pitch\textbackslash{}n(Pitch screen) specified by coarse tune (and fine tune).\textbackslash{}nPositive (“+”) settings make the pitch\textbackslash{}nhigher than the standard pitch; negative (“\sphinxhyphen{}”) settings make the pitch lower.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Level 4\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.TVF_FILTER_TYPE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(290, 0, 6, 0, 6, \textquotesingle{}Selects the type of filter. A filter cuts or boosts a specific frequency region to change a sound’s\textbackslash{}nbrightness, thickness, or other qualities.\textbackslash{}nOFF: The filter is not used.\textbackslash{}nLPF: Low Pass Filter. Cuts the portion that is above the cutoff frequency. By cutting the highfrequency\textbackslash{}nportion, this gives the sound a milder character. This is the most common filter\textbackslash{}nused in synthesizers.\textbackslash{}nBPF: Band Pass Filter. This leaves only the frequencies in the region of the cutoff frequency, and cuts the rest. This can be useful when creating distinctive sounds.\textbackslash{}nHPF: High Pass Filter. This cuts the frequencies in the region below the cutoff frequency. This is suitable for creating percussive sounds emphasizing their higher ones.\textbackslash{}nPKG: Peaking Filter. This emphasizes the frequencies in the region of the cutoff frequency. This represents the “resonance” of a drum.\textbackslash{}nLPF2: Low Pass Filter 2. Although frequency components above the cutoff frequency are cut, the sensitivity of this filter is half that of the LPF. This makes it a comparatively warmer low pass filter. This filter is good for use with simulated instrument sounds such as the acoustic piano.\textbackslash{}nLPF3: Low Pass Filter 3. Although frequency components above the cutoff frequency are cut, the sensitivity of this filter changes according to the Cutoff frequency. While this filter is also good for use with simulated acoustic instrument sounds, the nuance exhibits\textbackslash{}ndiffers from that of the LPF2, even with the same TVF Envelope settings\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam.TVF_CUTOFF_FREQUENCY}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(291, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Selects the frequency at which the filter begins to have an effect on the waveform’s frequency\textbackslash{}ncomponents.\textbackslash{}nWith “LPF/LPF2/LPF3” selected for the Filter Type parameter, lower cutoff frequency settings\textbackslash{}nreduce a tone’s upper harmonics for a more rounded, warmer sound. Higher settings make it\textbackslash{}nsound brighter.\textbackslash{}nIf the Filter Type parameter is set to “BPF,” the cutoff frequency setting changes the region of\textbackslash{}nharmonics that are allowed to sound. This can be useful when creating distinctive sounds.\textbackslash{}nWith “HPF” selected, higher Cutoff Frequency settings will reduce lower harmonics to emphasize\textbackslash{}njust the brighter components of the sound.\textbackslash{}nWith “PKG” selected, the harmonics to be emphasized will vary depending on Cutoff Frequency\textbackslash{}nsetting.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.partial.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(292, 0, 7, 0, 7, \textquotesingle{}Selects one of the following seven curves that determine how keyboard playing dynamics\textbackslash{}n(velocity) influence the cutoff frequency. Set this to “FIXED” if you don’t want the Cutoff\textbackslash{}nfrequency to be affected by the keyboard velocity.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\pysigline

\index{ASSIGN\_TYPE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{ASSIGN\_TYPE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.ASSIGN_TYPE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ASSIGN\_TYPE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(12, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}Assign Type sets the way sounds are played when the same key is pressed a number of times.\textbackslash{}nMULTI: Layer the sound of the same keys. Even with continuous sounds where the sound plays\textbackslash{}nfor an extended time, such as with crash cymbals, the sounds are layered, without previously\textbackslash{}nplayed sounds being eliminated.\textbackslash{}nSINGLE: Only one sound can be played at a time when the same key is pressed. With continuous\textbackslash{}nsounds where the sound plays for an extended time, the previous sound is stopped when the\textbackslash{}nfollowing sound is played.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{MUTE\_GROUP (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{MUTE\_GROUP}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.MUTE_GROUP}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{MUTE\_GROUP}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(13, 0, 31, 0, 31, \textquotesingle{}On an actual acoustic drum set, an open hi\sphinxhyphen{}hat and a closed hi\sphinxhyphen{}hat sound can never occur\textbackslash{}nsimultaneously.\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}nTo reproduce the reality of this situation, you can set up a Mute Group.\textbackslash{}nThe Mute Group function allows you to designate two or more drum partials that are not allowed\textbackslash{}nto sound simultaneously. Up to 31 Mute Groups can be used.\textbackslash{}nDrum partials that does not belong to any such group should be set to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_LEVEL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_LEVEL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_LEVEL}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_LEVEL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(14, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the volume of the waveform.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe volume of each drum partial is specified by TVA Level (p. 24), and the overall volume of\textbackslash{}nthe entire drum kit is specified by COMMON Kit Level (p. 20).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_COARSE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(15, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in semitone steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{} 4 octaves).\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall coarse tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Coarse Tune (p. 22).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_FINE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(16, 14, 114, \sphinxhyphen{}50, 50, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in 1\sphinxhyphen{}cent steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{}50 cents).\textbackslash{}n* One cent is 1/100th of a semitone.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall fine tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Fine Tune (p. 22 of maanual).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PITCH\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(17, 0, 30, 0, 30, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the width of random pitch deviation that will occur each time a key is pressed. If you do\textbackslash{}nnot want the pitch to change randomly, set this to “0.” These values are in units of cents (1/100th\textbackslash{}n of a semitone).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_PAN (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_PAN}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_PAN}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_PAN}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(18, 0, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}64, 63, \textquotesingle{}Sets the pan for the drum partial. “L64” is far left, “0” is center, and “63R” is far right.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nUse the Wave Pan parameter (p. 21 f the Manual) to specify the panning of the waveforms that make up\textbackslash{}nthe drum partial.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.PARTIAL_RANDOM_PAN_DEPTH}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_RANDOM\_PAN\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(19, 0, 63, 0, 63, \textquotesingle{}When a loop waveform is selected, the sound will normally continue as long as the key is\textbackslash{}npressed. If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to\textbackslash{}nSUSTAIN.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{PARTIAL\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(20, 1, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}63, 63, \textquotesingle{}Use this parameter when you want the stereo location to change randomly each time you press\textbackslash{}na key.\textbackslash{}nHigher settings will produce a greater amount of change.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nThis applies only to waves whose Wave Random Pan Sw parameter (p. 21) is ON.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{PARTIAL\_ENV\_MODE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{PARTIAL\_ENV\_MODE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to“SUSTAIN.” NO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS, SUSTAIN\textquotesingle{}, 127, None, None)}}}

\index{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}
