{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT\_VELOCITY\_CONTROL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(32, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}WMT Velocity Control determines whether a different drum partial is played (ON) or not (OFF)\textbackslash{}ndepending on the force with which the key is played (velocity).\textbackslash{}nWhen set to “RANDOM,” the drum kit’s constituent drum partials will sound randomly,\textbackslash{}n\textbackslash{}nregardless of any Velocity messages.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to“SUSTAIN.” NO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS, SUSTAIN\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_COLOR (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_COLOR}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_COLOR}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(49, 0, 3, 1, 4, \textquotesingle{}Specifies how FXM will perform frequency modulation.\textbackslash{}nHigher settings result in a grainier sound, while lower settings result in a more metallic sound.\textbackslash{}nValues: 1 \sphinxhyphen{} 4\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(50, 0, 16, 0, 16, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the depth of the modulation produced by FXM.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nIf Wave Tempo Sync is “ON,” pitch\sphinxhyphen{}related settings (p. 22) and FMX\sphinxhyphen{}related settings have no\textbackslash{}neffect.\textbackslash{}nValues: 0 \sphinxhyphen{} 16\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_WAVE_TEMPO_SYNC}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(51, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes the pitch of the waveform to be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “ON,” the pitch of the waveform will be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “OFF,” the pitch of the waveform will not be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_COARSE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_COARSE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_COARSE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(52, 16, 112, \sphinxhyphen{}48, 48, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in semitone steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{} 4 octaves).\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall coarse tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Coarse Tune (p. 22).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_FINE\_TUNE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_FINE\_TUNE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_FINE\_TUNE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(53, 14, 114, \sphinxhyphen{}50, 50, \textquotesingle{}Adjusts the pitch of the waveform’s sound up or down in 1/100th of a semitone steps (+/\sphinxhyphen{} 50 cents).\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe overall fine tuning for all of the drum partials is specified by PITCH Fine Tune (p. 22).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_PAN}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(54, 0, 127, \sphinxhyphen{}64, 63, \textquotesingle{}Sets the pan for the drum partial. “L64” is far left, “0” is center, and “63R” is far right.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nUse the Wave Pan parameter (p. 21 f the Manual) to specify the panning of the waveforms that make up\textbackslash{}nthe drum partial.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_WAVE_RANDOM_PAN_SWITCH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_RANDOM\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(55, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}Use this setting to cause the waveform’s panning to change randomly each time a key is pressed\textbackslash{}n(ON) or not (OFF).\textbackslash{}n* To specify the range in which pan is changed, edit Random Pan Depth (p. 24).\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_WAVE_ALTERNATE_PAN_SWITCH}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(56, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes panning of the waveform to be alternated between left and right each time a\textbackslash{}nkey is pressed.\textbackslash{}nSet Alter Pan Sw to “ON” to pan the Wave according to the Alter Pan Depth settings, or to\textbackslash{}n“REVS” when you want the panning reversed.\textbackslash{}nIf you do not want the panning to change each time a key is pressed, set this to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_WAVE_LEVEL}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_WAVE\_LEVEL}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(57, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the volume of the waveform.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}nThe volume of each drum partial is specified by TVA Level (p. 24), and the overall volume of\textbackslash{}nthe entire drum kit is specified by COMMON Kit Level (p. 20).\textbackslash{}nValues: 0 \sphinxhyphen{} 127\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_LOWER (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_LOWER}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_VELOCITY_RANGE_LOWER}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_LOWER}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(58, 1, 127, 1, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the lowest velocity at which the waveform will sound. Make these settings when you want\textbackslash{}ndifferent waveforms to sound in response to notes played at different strengths.\textbackslash{}nNOTE\textbackslash{}nIf you attempt to set the lower key velocity above the higher, or the higher key velocity\textbackslash{}nbelow the lower, the other setting changes in tandem.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_UPPER (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_UPPER}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_RANGE\_UPPER}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(59, 1, 127, 1, 127, \textquotesingle{}Sets the highest velocity at which the waveform will sound. Make these settings when you want\textbackslash{}ndifferent waveforms to sound in response to notes played at different strengths.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_LOWER (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_LOWER}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_LOWER}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(60, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Determines what will happen to the tone’s level when the tone is played at a velocity lower than\textbackslash{}nits specified velocity range. Higher settings produce a more gradual change in volume. If you\textbackslash{}nwant notes played outside the specified key velocity range to not be sounded at all, set this to 0\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_UPPER (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_UPPER}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.WMT1_VELOCITY_FADE_WIDTH_UPPER}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT1\_VELOCITY\_FADE\_WIDTH\_UPPER}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(61, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}This determines what will happen to the tone’s level when the tone is played at a velocity greater\textbackslash{}nthan its specified velocity range.\textbackslash{}nHigher settings produce a more gradual change in volume.\textbackslash{}nIf you want notes played outside the specified key velocity range to not be sounded at all, set\textbackslash{}nthis to 0\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

If you want the sound to decay naturally even if the key remains pressed, set this to\textbackslash{}nNO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS.\textbackslash{}nMEMO\textbackslash{}n* If a one\sphinxhyphen{}shot type Wave is selected, it will not sustain even if this parameter is set to“SUSTAIN.” NO\sphinxhyphen{}SUS, SUSTAIN\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{WMT2\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT2\_WAVE\_FXM\_DEPTH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\index{WMT2\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT2\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT2\_WAVE\_TEMPO\_SYNC}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(80, 0, 1, 0, 1, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes the pitch of the waveform to be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “ON,” the pitch of the waveform will be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textbackslash{}nWhen this is set to “OFF,” the pitch of the waveform will not be adjusted in response to the tempo of the song.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{WMT2\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{WMT2\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT2\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(85, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes panning of the waveform to be alternated between left and right each time a\textbackslash{}nkey is pressed.\textbackslash{}nSet Alter Pan Sw to “ON” to pan the Wave according to the Alter Pan Depth settings, or to\textbackslash{}n“REVS” when you want the panning reversed.\textbackslash{}nIf you do not want the panning to change each time a key is pressed, set this to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{WMT4\_WAVE\_ALTERNATE\_PAN\_SWITCH}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(271, 0, 2, 0, 2, \textquotesingle{}This setting causes panning of the waveform to be alternated between left and right each time a\textbackslash{}nkey is pressed.\textbackslash{}nSet Alter Pan Sw to “ON” to pan the Wave according to the Alter Pan Depth settings, or to\textbackslash{}n“REVS” when you want the panning reversed.\textbackslash{}nIf you do not want the panning to change each time a key is pressed, set this to “OFF.”\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}

\index{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.TVF_FILTER_TYPE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_FILTER\_TYPE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(290, 0, 6, 0, 6, \textquotesingle{}Selects the type of filter. A filter cuts or boosts a specific frequency region to change a sound’s\textbackslash{}nbrightness, thickness, or other qualities.\textbackslash{}nOFF: The filter is not used.\textbackslash{}nLPF: Low Pass Filter. Cuts the portion that is above the cutoff frequency. By cutting the highfrequency\textbackslash{}nportion, this gives the sound a milder character. This is the most common filter\textbackslash{}nused in synthesizers.\textbackslash{}nBPF: Band Pass Filter. This leaves only the frequencies in the region of the cutoff frequency, and cuts the rest. This can be useful when creating distinctive sounds.\textbackslash{}nHPF: High Pass Filter. This cuts the frequencies in the region below the cutoff frequency. This is suitable for creating percussive sounds emphasizing their higher ones.\textbackslash{}nPKG: Peaking Filter. This emphasizes the frequencies in the region of the cutoff frequency. This represents the “resonance” of a drum.\textbackslash{}nLPF2: Low Pass Filter 2. Although frequency components above the cutoff frequency are cut, the sensitivity of this filter is half that of the LPF. This makes it a comparatively warmer low pass filter. This filter is good for use with simulated instrument sounds such as the acoustic piano.\textbackslash{}nLPF3: Low Pass Filter 3. Although frequency components above the cutoff frequency are cut, the sensitivity of this filter changes according to the Cutoff frequency. While this filter is also good for use with simulated acoustic instrument sounds, the nuance exhibits\textbackslash{}ndiffers from that of the LPF2, even with the same TVF Envelope settings\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam.TVF_CUTOFF_FREQUENCY}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_CUTOFF\_FREQUENCY}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(291, 0, 127, 0, 127, \textquotesingle{}Selects the frequency at which the filter begins to have an effect on the waveform’s frequency\textbackslash{}ncomponents.\textbackslash{}nWith “LPF/LPF2/LPF3” selected for the Filter Type parameter, lower cutoff frequency settings\textbackslash{}nreduce a tone’s upper harmonics for a more rounded, warmer sound. Higher settings make it\textbackslash{}nsound brighter.\textbackslash{}nIf the Filter Type parameter is set to “BPF,” the cutoff frequency setting changes the region of\textbackslash{}nharmonics that are allowed to sound. This can be useful when creating distinctive sounds.\textbackslash{}nWith “HPF” selected, higher Cutoff Frequency settings will reduce lower harmonics to emphasize\textbackslash{}njust the brighter components of the sound.\textbackslash{}nWith “PKG” selected, the harmonics to be emphasized will vary depending on Cutoff Frequency\textbackslash{}nsetting.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_CURVE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(292, 0, 7, 0, 7, \textquotesingle{}Selects one of the following seven curves that determine how keyboard playing dynamics\textbackslash{}n(velocity) influence the cutoff frequency. Set this to “FIXED” if you don’t want the Cutoff\textbackslash{}nfrequency to be affected by the keyboard velocity.\textquotesingle{}, None)}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_SENS (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute)@\spxentry{TVF\_CUTOFF\_VELOCITY\_SENS}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.drum.DrumPartialParam attribute}}

\pysigline

\subparagraph{EffectParameter Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.effects:effectparameter-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{EffectParameter} class, which manages effect\sphinxhyphen{}related parameters
for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi patch data. It provides functionality to retrieve parameter addresses, convert
values between digital and MIDI formats, and categorize parameters based on effect types.

\subparagraph{Usage Example}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.effects:usage-example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{n}{param} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{EffectParameter}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}by\PYGZus{}name}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{EFX1\PYGZus{}LEVEL}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{k}{if} \PYG{n}{param}\PYG{p}{:}
    \PYG{n}{midi\PYGZus{}value} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{param}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{convert\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}midi}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mi}{64}\PYG{p}{)}
    \PYG{n+nb}{print}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+sa}{f}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{MIDI Value: }\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZob{}}\PYG{n}{midi\PYGZus{}value}\PYG{l+s+si}{\PYGZcb{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Function        Address Value Type      Description
EFX2 Type       00 00   0\textendash{}5 or 0\textendash{}8      Sets effect type (e.g., Thru, Distortion, Fuzz)
EFX2 Level      00 01   0\textendash{}127   Overall effect output
EFX2 Delay Send Level   00 02   0\textendash{}127   Send to Delay effect
EFX2 Reverb Send Level  00 03   0\textendash{}127   Send to Reverb effect
EFX2 Parameter 1        00 11   Signed 4\sphinxhyphen{}byte int       Meaning depends on EFX2 Type
EFX2 Parameter 2        00 15   Signed 4\sphinxhyphen{}byte int       As above
…     …     …     Up to Param 32 at 01 0D
Example for Distortion (EFX2\_TYPE = 01)
Param \# Address Meaning
Param 1 00 11   Drive
Param 2 00 15   Presence
Param 3 00 19   (possibly unused or Level)

\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.common}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-program-common-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.common}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.common}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{ProgramCommonParameter}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:programcommonparameter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the ProgramCommonParameter class for managing common program\sphinxhyphen{}level
parameters in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides attributes and methods for handling program\sphinxhyphen{}wide settings,
such as program name, level, tempo, and vocal effects. It also includes
methods for retrieving digital values, validating parameter values, and
handling partial\sphinxhyphen{}specific addressing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Create an instance for Program Level
program\_level = ProgramCommonParameter({\color{red}\bfseries{}*}ProgramCommonParameter.PROGRAM\_LEVEL)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate a value within range
validated\_value = program\_level.validate\_value(100)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get the digital name of a parameter
display\_name = program\_level.display\_name  \# “Program Level”

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get digital value range
display\_range = program\_level.get\_display\_value()  \# (0, 127)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Retrieve a parameter by name
param = ProgramCommonParameter.get\_by\_name(“PROGRAM\_TEMPO”)
if param:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
print(param.name, param.min\_val, param.max\_val)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get switch text representation
switch\_text = program\_level.get\_switch\_text(1)  \# “ON” or “—”

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.controller module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-program-controller-module}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-program-zone-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.program.zone}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{ProgramZoneParameter}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.program:programzoneparameter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Defines the ProgramZoneParameter class for managing common program\sphinxhyphen{}level
parameters in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides attributes and methods for handling program\sphinxhyphen{}wide settings,
such as program name, level, tempo, and vocal effects. It also includes
methods for retrieving digital values, validating parameter values, and
handling partial\sphinxhyphen{}specific addressing.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Create an instance for Program Level
program\_level = ProgramCommonParameter({\color{red}\bfseries{}*}ProgramCommonParameter.PROGRAM\_LEVEL)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate a value within range
validated\_value = program\_level.validate\_value(100)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get the digital name of a parameter
display\_name = program\_level.display\_name  \# “Program Level”

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get digital value range
display\_range = program\_level.get\_display\_value()  \# (0, 127)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Retrieve a parameter by name
param = ProgramCommonParameter.get\_by\_name(“PROGRAM\_TEMPO”)
if param:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
print(param.name, param.min\_val, param.max\_val)
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Get switch text representation
switch\_text = program\_level.get\_switch\_text(1)  \# “ON” or “—”

\subparagraph{Module: ArpeggioParameter}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:module-arpeggioparameter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the ArpeggioParameter class, which represents arpeggiator\sphinxhyphen{}related parameters
in a synthesizer. These parameters control various aspects of arpeggios, such as grid, duration,
style, motif, octave range, accent rate, and velocity, as well as pattern, rhythm, and note settings.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class provides methods to:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialize arpeggio parameters with a given address, range, and optional digital range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Store the minimum and maximum values for digital and parameter validation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Define a variety of arpeggio and pattern\sphinxhyphen{}related parameters with specific ranges, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio grid (e.g., {\color{red}\bfseries{}4\_}, {\color{red}\bfseries{}8\_}, {\color{red}\bfseries{}16\_})
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio duration (e.g., 30, 40, 50, 60)
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio style and motif
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio octave range (\sphinxhyphen{}3 to +3)
\sphinxhyphen{} Accent rate and velocity
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio pattern, rhythm, and note settings

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameters include:
\sphinxhyphen{} Arpeggio grid, duration, switch, style, motif, octave range, accent rate, and velocity.
\sphinxhyphen{} Pattern parameters (4 patterns, each with a range from 0 to 127).
\sphinxhyphen{} Rhythm parameters (4 rhythm settings, each with a range from 0 to 127).
\sphinxhyphen{} Note parameters (4 note settings, each with a range from 0 to 127).

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}\textasciigrave{}}{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}}python
Usage example:
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Initialize an arpeggio parameter object
param = ArpeggioParameter(address=0x01, min\_val=0, max\_val=8)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Access digital range values
log.message(param.display\_min)  \# Output: 0
log.message(param.display\_max)  \# Output: 8

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate a value for the parameter
valid\_value = param.validate\_value(5)
\end{quote}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_GRID (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_GRID}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam.ARPEGGIO_GRID}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ARPEGGIO\_GRID}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(1, 0, 8, 0, 8, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the time signature and “swing” of the arpeggio style.\textbackslash{}nThe setting specifies the note value that one grid unit represents, and the degree of shuffle (none, light, or heavy).\textbackslash{}n1/4: Eighth note (two grid sections = one beat)\textbackslash{}n1/8: Eighth note (two grid sections = one beat)\textbackslash{}n1/8L: Eighth note shuffle Light (two grid sections = one beat, with a light shuffle)\textbackslash{}n1/8H: Eighth note shuffle Heavy (two grid sections = one beat, with a heavy shuffle)\textbackslash{}n1/12: Eighth note triplet (three grid sections = one beat)\textbackslash{}n1/16: Sixteenth note (four grid sections = one beat)\textbackslash{}n1/16L: Sixteenth note shuffle Light (four grid sections = one beat, with a light shuffle)\textbackslash{}n1/16H: Sixteenth note shuffle Heavy (four grid sections = one beat, with a heavy shuffle)\textbackslash{}n1/24: Sixteenth note triplet (six grid sections = one beat)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Grid\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_DURATION (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_DURATION}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam.ARPEGGIO_DURATION}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ARPEGGIO\_DURATION}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(2, 0, 9, 0, 9, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the duration that each note of the arpeggio is sounded.\textbackslash{}nThis determines whether the sounds are played staccato (short and clipped), or tenuto (fully drawn out).\textbackslash{}n30\textendash{}120: For example if you specify “30,” each note on the grid (or in the case of tied notes, the last tied note) has a\textbackslash{}nduration that is 30\% of the note value specified by the grid.\textbackslash{}nFull: Even if the linked grid is not connected with a tie, the same note continues to sound until the point at which the\textbackslash{}nnext new sound is specified.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Duration\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_MOTIF (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_MOTIF}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam.ARPEGGIO_MOTIF}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ARPEGGIO\_MOTIF}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(6, 0, 11, 0, 11, \textquotesingle{}Value Explanation\textbackslash{}nUp (L) Only the lowest of the keys pressed is sounded each time, and the notes play in order from the lowest of the pressed keys.\textbackslash{}nUp (L\&H) Notes from both the lowest and highest pressed keys are sounded each time, and the notes play in order from the lowest of the pressed keys.\textbackslash{}nUp (\_) The notes play in order from the lowest of the pressed keys.\textbackslash{}nDown (L) Only the lowest of the keys pressed is sounded each time, and the notes play in order from the highest of the pressed keys.\textbackslash{}nDown (L\&H) Notes from both the lowest and highest pressed keys are sounded each time, and the notes play in order from the highest of the pressed keys.\textbackslash{}nDown (\_) The notes play in order from the highest of the pressed keys. No note is played every time.\textbackslash{}nU/D (L) Notes will be sounded from the lowest to the highest key you press and then back down to the lowest key, with only the lowest key sounded each time.\textbackslash{}nU/D (L\&H) Notes from both the lowest and highest pressed keys are sounded each time, and the notes play in order from the lowest of the pressed keys and then back\textbackslash{}nagain in the reverse order.\textbackslash{}nU/D (\_) The notes play in order from the lowest of the pressed keys, and then back again in the reverse order.\textbackslash{}nRand (L) Notes will be sounded randomly for the keys you press, with only the lowest key sounded each time.\textbackslash{}nRand (\_) Only the lowest of the keys pressed is sounded each time, the notes you press will be sounded randomly.\textbackslash{}nPhrase Pressing just one key will play a phrase based on the pitch of that key. If you press more than one key, the key you press last will be used.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Motif\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_OCTAVE\_RANGE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_OCTAVE\_RANGE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam.ARPEGGIO_OCTAVE_RANGE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ARPEGGIO\_OCTAVE\_RANGE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(7, 61, 67, 61, 67, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the range by which the arpeggio is shifted.\textbackslash{}nThis adds an effect that shifts arpeggios one cycle at a time in octave units (octave range).\textbackslash{}nYou can set the shift range upwards or downwards (up to three octaves up or down).\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Octave Range\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_ACCENT\_RATE (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_ACCENT\_RATE}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam.ARPEGGIO_ACCENT_RATE}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{ARPEGGIO\_ACCENT\_RATE}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }(9, 0, 100, 0, 100, \textquotesingle{}Specifies the accent strength for the arpeggio.\textbackslash{}nWith a setting of “100,” the arpeggiated notes will have the velocities that are programmed by the arpeggio style.\textbackslash{}nWith a setting of “0,” all arpeggiated notes will be sounded at a fixed velocity.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}Accent\textquotesingle{})}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\end{fulllineitems}

\index{ARPEGGIO\_VELOCITY (jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute)@\spxentry{ARPEGGIO\_VELOCITY}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.arpeggio.ArpeggioParam attribute}}

\pysigline

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.synth module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-synth-module}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal\_fx module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:module-jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal_fx}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:jdxi-editor-midi-data-parameter-vocal-fx-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal\_fx@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal\_fx}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal\_fx@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.vocal\_fx}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: VocalFXParameter}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.data.parameter:module-vocalfxparameter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the VocalFXParameter class, which represents various vocal effects parameters
in a synthesizer. These parameters control different aspects of vocal processing, including
level, pan, delay/reverb send levels, auto pitch settings, vocoder effects, and more.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class provides methods to:
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initialize vocal FX parameters with a given address, range, and optional digital range.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Validate and convert parameter values to the MIDI range (0\sphinxhyphen{}127).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Define a variety of vocal effects parameters with specific ranges, including:
\sphinxhyphen{} Level, pan, delay/reverb send levels, and output assignment
\sphinxhyphen{} Auto pitch settings such as switch, type, scale, key, note, gender, octave, and balance
\sphinxhyphen{} Vocoder parameters such as switch, envelope type, level, mic sensitivity, and mix level

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class also offers conversion utilities:
\sphinxhyphen{} Convert between MIDI values and digital values.
\sphinxhyphen{} Handle special bipolar cases (e.g., pan, auto pitch gender).
\sphinxhyphen{} Retrieve the digital value range or MIDI value range for parameters.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameters include:
\sphinxhyphen{} Level, pan, delay and reverb send levels, output assignment, and auto pitch settings
\sphinxhyphen{} Vocoder settings for on/off, envelope, level, mic sensitivity, synth level, and mic mix
\sphinxhyphen{} Auto pitch gender, octave, balance, and key/note configurations

\sphinxAtStartPar
The class also includes utility functions to get a parameter’s address, range, digital range,
and to convert between MIDI values and digital values.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Initialize a VocalFXParameter object for the LEVEL parameter
param = VocalFXParameter(address=0x00, min\_val=0, max\_val=127)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Access digital range values
print(param.display\_min)  \# Output: 0
print(param.display\_max)  \# Output: 127

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Validate a MIDI value
midi\_value = param.convert\_to\_midi(64)

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.connection module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.connection}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-connection-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.connection@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.connection}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.connection@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.connection}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDI Connection Singleton for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{MIDIConnection} class, which serves as a singleton for managing
MIDI input and output connections with the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer. It provides methods
for sending MIDI messages, setting input callbacks, and identifying connected devices.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Implements a singleton pattern to ensure a single MIDI connection instance.
\sphinxhyphen{} Manages MIDI input and output ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Sends MIDI messages with an optional UI indicator blink.
\sphinxhyphen{} Sets a callback function for incoming MIDI messages.
\sphinxhyphen{} Sends an Identity Request to detect and verify the connected device.
\sphinxhyphen{} Retrieves firmware version information from the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{rtmidi} for MIDI communication.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{logging} for debugging and error handling.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{DeviceInfo} and \sphinxtitleref{IdentityRequest} for device identification.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Example Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_conn = MIDIConnection()
midi\_conn.initialize(midi\_in, midi\_out, main\_window)
midi\_conn.send\_message({[}0x90, 0x40, 0x7F{]})  \# Send a Note On message
if midi\_conn.identify\_device():
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
print(“Connected to JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi:”, midi\_conn.device\_version)
\end{quote}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.controller module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.controller}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-controller-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.controller@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.controller}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.controller@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.controller}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDI I/O Controller for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the \sphinxtitleref{MidiIOController} class, which facilitates MIDI communication
with the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer. It allows users to list, open, and manage MIDI input
and output ports, automatically detect JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi ports, and handle MIDI message reception.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Retrieve available MIDI input and output ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Automatically detect JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi MIDI ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Open and close MIDI input and output ports by name or index.
\sphinxhyphen{} Check the status of open MIDI ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Set a callback for incoming MIDI messages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{rtmidi} for MIDI communication.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{PyQt6.QtCore} for QObject\sphinxhyphen{}based structure.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Example Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
controller = MidiIOController()
controller.open\_ports(“JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi MIDI IN”, “JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi MIDI OUT”)
print(controller.current\_in\_port, controller.current\_out\_port)

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.delay}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-delay-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_editor.midi.io.delay
send midi messages with a delay so that the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi can process them correctly.

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.helper}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-helper-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{MIDI Helper Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:midi-helper-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides address unified helper class for MIDI communication with the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.
It integrates both MIDI input and output functionalities by combining the features of
the MIDIInHandler and MIDIOutHandler classes.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{MIDIHelper: A helper class that inherits from both MIDIInHandler and MIDIOutHandler,}
\sphinxAtStartPar
offering address consolidated interface for handling MIDI messages (including
SysEx messages in JSON format) for the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.

\end{description}

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtCore.Signal for Qt signal support.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_editor.midi.input\_handler.MIDIInHandler for handling incoming MIDI messages.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_editor.midi.output\_handler.MIDIOutHandler for handling outgoing MIDI messages.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}
\index{MidiIOHelper (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper)@\spxentry{MidiIOHelper}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Save patch as raw Roland SysEx (.syx) from JSON files in temp folder.
Reads each JSON file, converts to SysEx bytes, concatenates, and writes.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{file\_path}} \textendash{} str Output .syx file path

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{temp\_folder}} \textendash{} Path\sphinxhyphen{}like Folder containing JSON patch files (from json\_composer.process\_editor)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
bool True on success

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\index{load\_patch() (jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper.MidiIOHelper method)@\spxentry{load\_patch()}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.helper.MidiIOHelper method}}

{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load the patch file and send to the instrument.
Handles .jsz/.msz (JSON bundles), .json, and .syx (binary SysEx).
.msz bundles may contain MIDI files (handled by PatchManager).

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.input\_handler module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.input_handler}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-input-handler-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.input\_handler@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.input\_handler}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.input\_handler@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.input\_handler}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{MIDIInHandler Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:midiinhandler-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the MIDIInHandler class for handling MIDI communication with the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.
It supports processing of SysEx, Control Change, Program Change, Note On/Off, and Clock messages.
The handler decodes incoming MIDI messages, routes them to appropriate sub\sphinxhyphen{}handlers, and emits Qt signals
for integration with PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based applications.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{MIDIInHandler: Processes incoming MIDI messages, handles SysEx tone data parsing, and emits signals}
\sphinxAtStartPar
for further processing in the application.

\end{description}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Instantiate MIDIInHandler and connect to its signals to integrate MIDI data handling with your application.

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtCore.Signal for signal emission.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
pubsub for publish/subscribe messaging.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager modules for data handling, parsing, and MIDI processing.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Helper class for MIDI communication with the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class listens to incoming MIDI messages, processes them based on
their preset\_type, and emits corresponding signals. It handles SysEx, Control
Change, Program Change, Note On/Off, and Clock messages.

mido doesn’t have callbacks, so we convert
:param message: list{[}Any{]}
:param data: Any

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:module-jdxi_editor.midi.io.output_handler}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:jdxi-editor-midi-io-output-handler-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{MIDI Output Handler}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.io:midi-output-handler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the \sphinxtitleref{MIDIOutHandler} class for managing MIDI output, allowing users to send
note\sphinxhyphen{}on, note\sphinxhyphen{}off, and control change messages through address specified MIDI output port.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
rtmidi: A library for working with MIDI messages and ports.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Example usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
handler = MIDIOutHandler(“MIDI Output Port”)
handler.send\_note\_on(60, velocity=100)
handler.send\_note\_off(60)
handler.send\_control\_change(7, 127)
handler.close()

\end{description}
\index{MidiOutHandler (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler)@\spxentry{MidiOutHandler}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.io.output\_handler}}

Ensures all values in the data list are 7\sphinxhyphen{}bit safe (0\textendash{}127).
Bytes \textgreater{} 127 are split into two 4\sphinxhyphen{}bit nibbles.
:param data: List of integer byte values (0\textendash{}255)
:return: List of 7\sphinxhyphen{}bit\sphinxhyphen{}safe integers (0\textendash{}127)

\subparagraph{DigitalToneMessage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas:digitaltonemessage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Example usage:
\# Set common parameter
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = DigitalToneMessage(
…     command=CommandID.DT1,  \# Digital 1
…     value=64,

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas:module-jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas.system_common}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas:jdxi-editor-midi-message-areas-system-common-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{SystemCommonMessage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas:systemcommonmessage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Example usage:
\# Set master tune to +50 cents
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} from jdxi\_editor.midi.data.parameter.system.common import SystemCommonParam
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = SystemCommonMessage(
…     lsb=SystemCommonParam.MASTER\_TUNE.value,
…     value=1024 + (50 * 10),  \# Convert +50.0 cents to 1524
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Set master key shift to \sphinxhyphen{}12 semitones
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = SystemCommonMessage(
…     lsb=SystemCommonParam.MASTER\_KEY\_SHIFT.value, value=52  \# Convert \sphinxhyphen{}12 to 52 (64\sphinxhyphen{}12)
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Set program control channel to 1
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = SystemCommonMessage(
…     lsb=SystemCommonParam.PROGRAM\_CTRL\_CH.value, value=1  \# Channel 1
… )

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Enable program change reception
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = SystemCommonMessage(lsb=SystemCommonParam.RX\_PROGRAM\_CHANGE.value, value=1)  \# ON
\index{SystemCommonMessage (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common)@\spxentry{SystemCommonMessage}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.message.areas.system\_common}}

\subparagraph{SystemControllerMessage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message.areas:systemcontrollermessage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Example usage:
\# Enable program change transmission
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg = SystemControllerMessage()
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg.to\_bytes().hex()
‘f041100000000e1202000300007bf7’
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} msg.to\_list()
{[}240, 65, \textless{}RolandID.DEVICE\_ID: 16\textgreater{}, 0, 0, 0, 14, \textless{}CommandID.DT1: 18\textgreater{}, \textless{}JDXiSysExAddressStartMSB.SETUP: 2\textgreater{}, \textless{}JDXiSysExOffsetTemporaryToneUMB.COMMON: 0\textgreater{}, \textless{}JDXiSysExOffsetSystemLMB.CONTROLLER: 3\textgreater{}, 0, 0, 123, 247{]}

\subparagraph{JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Model ID and Header List Constants}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:jd-xi-model-id-and-header-list-constants}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module contains constants for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi model IDs and header lists.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Constants Used:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ModelID: Model ID constants

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
RolandID: Roland ID constants

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage Example:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{[}\textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_4: 0x0E\textgreater{}{]}
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} JDXiSysexHeader.to\_list()
{[}\textless{}RolandID.ROLAND\_ID: 65\textgreater{}, \textless{}RolandID.DEVICE\_ID: 16\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_1: 0x00\textgreater{}, \textless{}ModelID.MODEL\_ID\_4: 0x0E\textgreater{}{]}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi System Exclusive Message Header

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides a structured way to work with JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi SysEx headers,
replacing the old JD\_XI\_HEADER\_LIST constant.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{header} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{JDXiSysexHeader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}list}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{header\PYGZus{}bytes} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{JDXiSysexHeader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{header\PYGZus{}len} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{len}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{JDXiSysexHeader}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}list}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.midi module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:module-jdxi_editor.midi.message.midi}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:jdxi-editor-midi-message-midi-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.message.midi@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.midi}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.midi@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.midi}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{MIDI Message Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:midi-message-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a base class for handling MIDI messages in a structured manner.
It defines the \sphinxtitleref{Message} class, which serves as the foundation for various types of
MIDI messages, ensuring proper formatting and conversion to MIDI\sphinxhyphen{}compliant byte sequences.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Message: Base class for MIDI messages, enforcing structure and conversion methods.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides constants for MIDI message handling (status mask, channel mask, max value).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Ensures subclass implementation of the \sphinxtitleref{to\_list} method for MIDI byte conversion.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Offers utility methods for converting messages to bytes and hexadecimal string format.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage Example:}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k}{class}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nc}{NoteOnMessage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{MidiMessage}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{k}{def}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nf}{to\PYGZus{}list}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n+nb+bp}{self}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{:}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }        \PYG{k}{return} \PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mh}{0x90}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{60}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{127}\PYG{p}{]}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} Note On for Middle C with velocity 127}
\PYG{g+gp}{...}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msg} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{NoteOnMessage}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msg}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}bytes}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{b\PYGZsq{}\PYGZlt{}\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msg}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{to\PYGZus{}hex\PYGZus{}string}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}90 3C 7F\PYGZsq{}}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}

\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:module-jdxi_editor.midi.message.roland}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:jdxi-editor-midi-message-roland-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.message.roland@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.roland}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.roland@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.message.roland}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{roland\_sysex.py}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.message:roland-sysex-py}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a class for constructing and parsing Roland System Exclusive (SysEx) messages. The \sphinxtitleref{RolandSysEx} class allows for easy creation of messages to be sent to Roland devices, as well as the ability to parse incoming SysEx messages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Usage Example:

\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}\textasciigrave{}}{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}}python
from roland\_sysex import RolandSysEx

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Creating a SysEx message
message = RolandSysEx(command=0x12, area=0x01, section=0x02, group=0x03, param=0x04, value=0x05)
message\_bytes = message.to\_bytes()
print(“Generated SysEx Message (bytes):”, message\_bytes)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Parsing a SysEx message from bytes
received\_bytes = b’ðA÷’  \# Example received SysEx message
parsed\_message = RolandSysEx.from\_bytes(received\_bytes)
print(“Parsed Command:”, parsed\_message.command)
print(“Parsed Address:”, parsed\_message.address)
print(“Parsed Value:”, parsed\_message.value)

\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi specialized class for Roland SysEx messages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Inherits from PicoMidi’s generic RolandSysExMessage and adds
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi specific features like address object handling,
automatic value conversion, and JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi validation.

New code should import directly from picomidi.rpn.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Module: parameter\_handler

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{ParameterHandler} class, which manages MIDI parameter values
and emits signals when parameters are updated.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ParameterHandler: Handles storing, updating, retrieving, and clearing MIDI parameters.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Signals:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
parameters\_updated: Emitted when parameter values change.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.program.helper module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:module-jdxi_editor.midi.program.helper}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:jdxi-editor-midi-program-helper-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.program.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.program.helper}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.program.helper@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.program.helper}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: program\_handler}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:module-program-handler}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{PresetHandler} class, which extends \sphinxtitleref{PresetLoader} to manage
preset selection and navigation for a MIDI\sphinxhyphen{}enabled synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Classes:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:classes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{PresetHandler}: Handles preset loading, switching, and signaling for UI updates.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:dependencies}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{PySide6.QtCore} (Signal, QObject) for event\sphinxhyphen{}driven UI interaction.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.presets.type.PresetType} for managing preset types.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.preset.loader.PresetLoader} as the base class for preset loading.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Functionality:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:functionality}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Loads presets via MIDI.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Emits signals when a preset changes (\sphinxtitleref{preset\_changed}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports navigation through available presets (\sphinxtitleref{next\_tone}, \sphinxtitleref{previous\_tone}).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieves current preset details (\sphinxtitleref{get\_current\_preset}).

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.program:usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This class is typically used within a larger MIDI control application to handle
preset changes and communicate them to the UI and MIDI engine.

\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:module-jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:jdxi-editor-midi-sysex-parser-sysex-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sysex parser
\# Example usage:
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} sysex\_data = bytes({[}0xF0, 0x41, 0x10, 0x00, 0x00, 0x00, 0x0E, 0x12, 0x19, 0x01, 0x00, 0x00, 0x10, 0x7F, 0x57, 0xF7{]})
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} parser = JDXiSysExParser(sysex\_data)
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} result = parser.parse()
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} print(result)
\{‘JD\_XI\_HEADER’: ‘f041100000000e’, ‘ADDRESS’: ‘12190100’, ‘TEMPORARY\_AREA’: ‘DIGITAL\_SYNTH\_1’, ‘SYNTH\_TONE’: ‘COMMON’, ‘TONE\_NAME’: ‘Unknown’, ‘TONE\_NAME\_1’: 16, ‘TONE\_NAME\_2’: 127, ‘TONE\_NAME\_3’: 87, ‘TONE\_NAME\_4’: 247, ‘TONE\_NAME\_5’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_6’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_7’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_8’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_9’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_10’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_11’: 0, ‘TONE\_NAME\_12’: 0, ‘TONE\_LEVEL’: 0, ‘PORTAMENTO\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘PORTAMENTO\_TIME’: 0, ‘MONO\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘OCTAVE\_SHIFT’: 0, ‘PITCH\_BEND\_UP’: 0, ‘PITCH\_BEND\_DOWN’: 0, ‘PARTIAL1\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘PARTIAL1\_SELECT’: 0, ‘PARTIAL2\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘PARTIAL2\_SELECT’: 0, ‘PARTIAL3\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘PARTIAL3\_SELECT’: 0, ‘RING\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘UNISON\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘PORTAMENTO\_MODE’: 0, ‘LEGATO\_SWITCH’: 0, ‘ANALOG\_FEEL’: 0, ‘WAVE\_SHAPE’: 0, ‘TONE\_CATEGORY’: 0, ‘UNISON\_SIZE’: 0\}
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} isinstance(result, dict)
True
\index{JDXiSysExParser (class in jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex)@\spxentry{JDXiSysExParser}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex.JDXiSysExParser}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{class\DUrole{w}{ }}}\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{JDXiSysExParser}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{sysex\_data}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#bytes}{bytes}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/constants.html\#None}{None}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{None}}}
{}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Bases: \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#object}{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{object}}}

\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi System Exclusive Message Parser

\sphinxAtStartPar
Parses JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi SysEx messages following a structure similar to Picomidi’s Parser pattern.
Handles identity\_request requests, parameter messages (short and long), and message conversion.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The parser leverages field mappings defined in JDXiSysExParameterLayout.FIELDS to provide
structured parsing of SysEx messages. Use get\_structured\_fields() to extract parsed field data.
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msg\PYGZus{}hex} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{F0 41 10 00 00 00 0E 12 19 42 00 00 48 6F 75 73 65 20 42 61 73 73 20 31 00 01 37 00 00 11 40 40 40 01 02 40 40 00 7F 40 7F 7F 40 01 01 7F 40 00 40 00 0A 1E 00 7F 5C 40 5E 00 7F 00 00 00 14 00 40 00 02 00 50 40 40 52 00 00 00 00 05 F7}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msg} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{bytes}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{fromhex}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{msg\PYGZus{}hex}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{replace}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{ }\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{)}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{parser} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{JDXiSysExParser}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{msg}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{fields} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{parser}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{get\PYGZus{}structured\PYGZus{}fields}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{roland\PYGZus{}id} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{fields}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{roland\PYGZus{}id}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}  \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} RolandID enum member}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{address} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{fields}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{address}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}      \PYG{c+c1}{\PYGZsh{} ParameterAddress or bytes}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\index{get\_structured\_fields() (jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex.JDXiSysExParser method)@\spxentry{get\_structured\_fields()}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex.JDXiSysExParser method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser.sysex.JDXiSysExParser.get_structured_fields}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_structured\_fields}}}
{}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#dict}{dict}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Get structured field data using the field mappings.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This is a public method that can be used to extract structured data
from SysEx messages using the field specifications.
\subsubsection*{Example}

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:module-jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.parser:jdxi-editor-midi-sysex-parser-utils-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.parser.utils}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi SysEx Parser Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides functions to parse JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer SysEx data, extracting relevant tone parameters
for Digital, Analog, and Drum Kit sounds. It includes utilities for safely retrieving values, mapping
address bytes to synth areas, extracting tone names, and identifying tone types.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
safe\_get: Safely retrieves values from SysEx data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
extract\_hex: Extracts address hex value from SysEx data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
get\_temporary\_area: Maps SysEx address bytes to temporary areas.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
get\_synth\_tone: Maps byte values to synth tone types.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
extract\_tone\_name: Extracts and cleans the tone name from SysEx data.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
parse\_parameters: Parses JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi tone parameters for different synth types.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
parse\_sysex: Parses JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi tone data from SysEx messages.

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Safely retrieve values from SysEx data with an optional offset.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{data}} \textendash{} bytes SysEx message data

\item {}

{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Convert a 2\sphinxhyphen{}byte address (MSB, LSB) to a flat integer index.
For example, MSB=0x01, LSB=0x15 \(\rightarrow\) 0x0115 \(\rightarrow\) 277.
:param msb: int Most Significant Byte (0\textendash{}255)
:param lsb: int Least Significant Byte (0\textendash{}255)
:return: int address index

\end{fulllineitems}

\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.request:module-jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.request.factory}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.request:jdxi-editor-midi-sysex-request-factory-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.request.factory@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.request.factory}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.request.factory@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.request.factory}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module contains constants, functions, and logic to generate and compute
Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi SysEx messages for requesting program and tone names.

\sphinxAtStartPar
It defines a set of SysEx constants specific to the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi and provides the
following functionality:
\begin{enumerate}
\sphinxsetlistlabels{\arabic}{enumi}{enumii}{}{.}%
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Constants}: A dictionary \sphinxtitleref{SYSEX\_CONSTANTS} containing key constants
required to construct the SysEx messages, including the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi header,
tone areas, and other necessary fields.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Checksum Calculation}: A function \sphinxtitleref{roland\_checksum} that computes the
Roland checksum for a given SysEx data string. This checksum is used
to ensure the integrity of the message.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Request Generation}: A function \sphinxtitleref{create\_request} that dynamically generates
a SysEx message based on the header, tone area, and parameters provided.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstylestrong{Program and Tone Requests}: A list \sphinxtitleref{PROGRAM\_AND\_TONE\_NAME\_REQUESTS}
containing pre\sphinxhyphen{}generated SysEx requests for various program and tone areas
like digital, analog, and drums.

\end{enumerate}

\sphinxAtStartPar
SysEx message format includes:
\sphinxhyphen{} Start byte \sphinxtitleref{F0}, end byte \sphinxtitleref{F7}
\sphinxhyphen{} JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi specific header and command information
\sphinxhyphen{} Roland checksum for data integrity

\sphinxAtStartPar
Functions:
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{roland\_checksum(data: str) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} str}: Computes and returns the Roland checksum
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
based on the input SysEx data string.
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{create\_request(header, tone\_area, param1) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} str}: Constructs a complete
SysEx request message, appends the checksum, and returns the full message.
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To generate a request for the program common area:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{request} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{create\PYGZus{}request}\PYG{p}{(}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{TEMPORARY\PYGZus{}PROGRAM\PYGZus{}RQ11\PYGZus{}HEADER}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{n}{SYSEX\PYGZus{}CONSTANTS}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{l+s+s1}{PROGRAM\PYGZus{}COMMON\PYGZus{}AREA}\PYG{l+s+s1}{\PYGZsq{}}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{,}
\PYG{g+gp}{... }    \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{00 00 00 00 00 40}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+go}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\sphinxAtStartPar
To retrieve a list of all program and tone name requests:
all\_requests = PROGRAM\_AND\_TONE\_NAME\_REQUESTS
\end{quote}

\end{itemize}

\pysigstartsignatures

{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{header}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{temp\_area}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{p}{|}\DUrole{w}{ }{\hyperref[\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.request:jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.request.hex.JDXISysExHex}]{\sphinxcrossref{JDXISysExHex}}}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{part}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Create a SysEx request string using header, area, parameter, and Roland checksum.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The Roland RQ1 checksum must cover all bytes from the first byte after the
command (0x11) through the last data byte. The header’s final token is the
first address byte (e.g. 18 for TEMPORARY\_PROGRAM), so it must be included
in the checksum input.

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.device module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex:module-jdxi_editor.midi.sysex.device}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex:jdxi-editor-midi-sysex-device-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.device@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.device}}\index{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.device@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.midi.sysex.device}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Device Information Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DeviceInfo} class, which represents identity\_request information
for a Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer. It provides utilities for checking manufacturer
and model identity\_request, extracting firmware version, and constructing an instance
from a MIDI Identity Reply message.


\subparagraph{Usage Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex:usage-example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{identity\PYGZus{}data} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n+nb}{bytes}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{l+m+mh}{0xF0}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x7E}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x10}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x06}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x02}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x41}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x0E}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x03}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x03}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mh}{0xF7}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{device} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{DeviceInfo}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}identity\PYGZus{}reply}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{identity\PYGZus{}data}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{device} \PYG{o+ow}{is} \PYG{o+ow}{not} \PYG{k+kc}{None}
\PYG{g+go}{True}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{device}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{version\PYGZus{}string}
\PYG{g+go}{\PYGZsq{}v0.03\PYGZsq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{device}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{is\PYGZus{}jdxi}
\PYG{g+go}{True}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}


\subparagraph{Classes:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex:classes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
DeviceInfo: Represents JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi device information, including manufacturer, model,
and firmware version. Provides utility properties for checking if the device
is a JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi and formatting the version string.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Methods:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.sysex:methods}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxtitleref{from\_identity\_reply(data: bytes) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} Optional{[}DeviceInfo{]}}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parses a MIDI Identity Reply message to create a \sphinxtitleref{DeviceInfo} instance.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxtitleref{is\_roland \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} bool}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns \sphinxtitleref{True} if the device manufacturer is Roland.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxtitleref{is\_jdxi \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} bool}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns \sphinxtitleref{True} if the device is identified as a JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{\sphinxtitleref{version\_string \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} str}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Returns the firmware version as a formatted string.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\subparagraph{MIDI Waveform Types}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.midi.wave:midi-waveform-types}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{Waveform} enum, which represents the different waveform types available on the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Constants:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_SAW: Sawtooth waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_SQUARE: Square waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_TRIANGLE: Triangle waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_SINE: Sine waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_NOISE: Noise waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_SUPER\_SAW: Super saw waveform

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
OSC\_WAVE\_PCM: PCM waveform

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage Example:}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{waveform} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{Waveform}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{SAW}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{waveform}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{midi\PYGZus{}value}
\PYG{g+go}{0x00}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{Waveform}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{from\PYGZus{}midi\PYGZus{}value}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+m+mh}{0x00}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+go}{Waveform.SAW}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}
