\subparagraph{Module: analog\_synth\_editor}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.analog:module-analog-synth-editor}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{AnalogSynthEditor} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing analog synthesizer parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{SynthEditor} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.analog:key-features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying analog synth parameters, including
oscillator, filter, amp, LFO, and envelope settings.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports MIDI communication to send and receive real\sphinxhyphen{}time parameter changes.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Allows selection of different analog synth presets from a dropdown menu.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays an instrument image that updates based on the selected preset.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes a scrollable layout for managing a variety of parameter controls.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Implements bipolar parameter handling for proper UI representation.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports waveform selection with custom buttons and icons.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a “Send Read Request to Synth” button to retrieve current synth settings.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Enables MIDI\sphinxhyphen{}triggered updates via incoming program changes and parameter adjustments.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.analog:dependencies}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (Analog.Parameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.analog:usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{AnalogSynthEditor} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.analog:example}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_helper = MIDIHelper()
preset\_helper = PresetHandler()
editor = AnalogSynthEditor(midi\_helper, preset\_helper)
editor.show()
\end{quote}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-arpeggio-arpeggio-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.arpeggio.arpeggio}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arpeggio Editor Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioEditor} class, a specialized editor for configuring arpeggiator
settings within a synthesizer. It extends the \sphinxtitleref{SynthEditor} class, providing a user\sphinxhyphen{}friendly
interface to control various arpeggiator parameters.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ArpeggioEditor: A \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} subclass that allows users to modify arpeggiator parameters
such as style, grid, duration, velocity, accent, swing, octave range, and motif.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides an intuitive UI with labeled controls and dropdown menus for parameter selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes a toggle switch to enable or disable the arpeggiator.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays an instrument image for better user engagement.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses MIDI integration to send real\sphinxhyphen{}time parameter changes to the synthesizer.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports dynamic visualization and interaction through sliders and combo boxes.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}\textasciigrave{}}{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}}python
from PySide6.QtWidgets import QApplication
from midi\_helper import MIDIHelper

\sphinxAtStartPar
app = QApplication({[}{]})
midi\_helper = MIDIHelper()
editor = ArpeggioEditor(midi\_helper=midi\_helper)
editor.show()
app.exec()
{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}\textasciigrave{}}{\color{red}\bfseries{}\textasciigrave{}}

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ArpeggioParameter (for managing parameter addresses and value ranges)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Slider (for smooth control over numerical parameters)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Digital Synth Editor for the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the UI components for editing digital synth parameters on the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.
The editor supports three partials (voices) with individual parameter control and common parameters
that affect all partials.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{DigitalSynthEditor: Main editor class for digital synth parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Handles MIDI communication for parameter changes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Manages UI state for all digital synth controls

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides preset loading and management

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports real\sphinxhyphen{}time parameter updates via SysEx

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Three independent partial editors

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Common parameter controls (portamento, unison, legato, etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Preset management and loading

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Real\sphinxhyphen{}time MIDI parameter updates

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ADSR envelope controls for both amplitude and filter

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Oscillator waveform selection

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Partial enabling/disabling and selection

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 for UI components

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
qtawesome for icons

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Custom MIDI handling classes

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Digital synth parameter definitions

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_output}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-drum-output}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumOutputSection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum output parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:key-features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum output parameters, including
partial output level, partial chorus send level, partial reverb send level, and partial output assign.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:dependencies}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (AnalogParameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{DrumOutputSection} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:example}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_helper = MIDIHelper()
editor = DrumOutputSection(midi\_helper)
editor.show()
\end{quote}
\index{DrumOutputSection (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.output)@\spxentry{DrumOutputSection}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.output}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-drum-partial-pitch-module}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch_env}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-drum-partial-pitch-env-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_pitch\_env}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-drum-pitch-env}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumPitchEnvSection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum pitch envelope parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id1}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum pitch envelope parameters, including
pitch env depth, pitch env velocity sens, pitch env time1 velocity sens, pitch env time4 velocity sens,
pitch env time1, pitch env time2, pitch env time3, pitch env time4, pitch env level0, pitch env level1,
pitch env level2, pitch env level3, and pitch env level4.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes a visual envelope plot showing the 5\sphinxhyphen{}level, 4\sphinxhyphen{}time\sphinxhyphen{}segment envelope curve.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id2}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (AnalogParameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id3}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{DrumPitchEnvSection} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id4}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_helper = MIDIHelper()
editor = DrumPitchEnvSection(midi\_helper)
editor.show()
\end{quote}
\index{DrumPitchEnvSection (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env)@\spxentry{DrumPitchEnvSection}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.pitch\_env}}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_tva}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-drum-tva}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumTVASection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum TVA parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id5}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum TVA parameters, including
level velocity curve, level velocity sens, env time1 velocity sens, env time4 velocity sens,
env time1, env time2, env time3, env time4, env level1, env level2, env level3, and env level4.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id6}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (AnalogParameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{DrumTVASection} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.

editor = DrumTVASection(midi\_helper)

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-drum-partial-tvf-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_tvf}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-drum-tvf}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumTVFSection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum TVF parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id9}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum TVF parameters, including
filter type, cutoff frequency, cutoff velocity curve, env depth, env velocity curve type,
env velocity sens, env time1 velocity sens, env time4 velocity sens, env time1, env time2,
env time3, env time4, env level0, env level1, env level2, env level3, and env level4.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes a visual envelope plot showing the 5\sphinxhyphen{}level, 4\sphinxhyphen{}time\sphinxhyphen{}segment TVF envelope curve.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id10}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (AnalogParameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id11}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{DrumTVFSection} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id12}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_helper = MidiIOHelper()
editor = DrumTVFSection(midi\_helper)
editor.show()
\end{quote}
\index{DrumTVFSection (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf)@\spxentry{DrumTVFSection}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.tvf}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-drum-partial-wmt-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_wmt}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:module-drum-wmt}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumWMTSection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum WMT parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id13}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum WMT parameters, including
wave selection, gain, FXM color, depth, tempo sync, coarse tune, fine tune, pan,
random pan switch, alternate pan switch, velocity range lower, velocity range upper,
velocity fade width lower, velocity fade width upper, and wave level.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id14}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6 (for UI components and event handling)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIHelper (for handling MIDI communication)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PresetHandler (for managing synth presets)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Various custom enums and helper classes (AnalogParameter, AnalogCommonParameter, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id15}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
The \sphinxtitleref{DrumWMTSection} class can be instantiated as part of a larger PySide6 application.
It requires a \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance for proper communication with the synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial:id16}}\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_helper = MIDIHelper()
editor = DrumWMTSection(midi\_helper)
editor.show()
\end{quote}
\index{DrumWMTSection (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt)@\spxentry{DrumWMTSection}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.partial.wmt}}

\subparagraph{Module: drum\_common}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:module-drum-common}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{DrumCommonSection} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based
user interface for editing drum common parameters in the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.
It extends the \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} base class and integrates MIDI communication for real\sphinxhyphen{}time
parameter adjustments and preset management.


\subparagraph{Key Features:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:key-features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides a graphical editor for modifying drum common parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Currently includes Kit Level control (the primary common parameter for drum kits).

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Note: Tone name is handled by the instrument preset group, not this section.

\end{itemize}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-drum-editor-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.drum.editor}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{DrumEditor Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:drumeditor-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides the \sphinxtitleref{DrumEditor} class, which serves as
an editor for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Drum Kit parameters.
It enables users to modify drum kit settings,
select presets, and send MIDI messages to address connected JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.


\subparagraph{Classes}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:classes}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{DrumEditor}: A graphical editor for JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi drum kits, supporting preset

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
selection, parameter adjustments, and MIDI communication.


\subparagraph{Dependencies}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:dependencies}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{PySide6.QtWidgets} for UI components.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{PySide6.QtCore} for Qt core functionality.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.parameter.drums.DrumParameter} for drum parameter definitions.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.presets.data.DRUM\_PRESETS\_ENUMERATED} for enumerated drum presets.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.presets.preset\_type.PresetType} for preset categorization.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.io.MIDIHelper} for MIDI communication.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.preset.loader.PresetLoader} for loading JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi presets.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.ui.editors.drum\_partial.DrumPartialEditor} for managing individual drum partials.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.ui.style.Style} for UI styling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.ui.editors.base.SynthEditor} as the base class for the editor.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.constants.sysex.DIGITAL\_SYNTH\_1} for SysEx address handling.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.ui.widgets.preset.combo\_box.PresetComboBox} for preset selection.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Features}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:features}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays and edits JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi drum kit parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports drum kit preset selection and loading.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides sliders, spin boxes, and combo boxes for adjusting kit parameters.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes address tabbed interface for managing individual drum partials.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sends MIDI System Exclusive (SysEx) messages to update the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi in real time.

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.drum:usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
To use the \sphinxtitleref{DrumEditor}, instantiate it with an optional \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} instance:

\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{jdxi\PYGZus{}editor}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{midi}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{io}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{MIDIHelper}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{jdxi\PYGZus{}editor}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{ui}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{editors}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{drum\PYGZus{}editor}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{DrumEditor}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{PySide6}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{QtWidgets}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QApplication}

\PYG{n}{app} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{QApplication}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{[}\PYG{p}{]}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{midi\PYGZus{}helper} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{MIDIHelper}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{editor} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{DrumEditor}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{midi\PYGZus{}helper}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{editor}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{show}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{n}{app}\PYG{o}{.}\PYG{n}{exec}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.common module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects.common}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-effects-common-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.common}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.common@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.common}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module: effects\_editor

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{EffectsEditor} class, which provides a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based user interface
for editing effects parameters on the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer. It extends \sphinxtitleref{SynthEditor} and
allows users to modify various effects settings, including Effect 1, Effect 2, Delay, and Reverb.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
EffectsEditor: A QWidget subclass for managing and editing JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi effect parameters.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
os

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
logging

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtWidgets (etc.)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtCore (Qt)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtGui (QPixmap)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager modules for MIDI and parameter handling

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays effects parameters with interactive controls.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports updating instrument images dynamically.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sends MIDI messages to update effect settings in real\sphinxhyphen{}time.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Organizes effect parameters into categorized tabs.
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_17,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_18,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_19,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_20,

AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_24,

AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_28,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_29,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_30,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX1\_PARAM\_31,

\sphinxAtStartPar
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_17,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_18,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_19,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_20,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_21,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_22,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_23,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_24,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_25,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_26,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_27,
AddressParameterEffect1.EFX2\_PARAM\_28,

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

Override showEvent to request current settings from the instrument when the editor is shown.
This ensures the sliders pick up the current settings from the instrument, similar to
Digital 1, Digital 2, and Analog synth editors.

\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}

\textendash{} D0:100W)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_GENDER\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Formant shift (\sphinxhyphen{}10 to +10)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_KEY\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Key (C, Db, D, … Bm)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_NOTE\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Root note (C, C\#, D, … B)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_OCTAVE\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Octave shift (\sphinxhyphen{}1 to +1)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_SCALE\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Scale: CHROMATIC or Maj(Min)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_SWITCH\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Enable or bypass auto pitch\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}AUTO\_PITCH\_TYPE\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Auto pitch character: SOFT, HARD, ELECTRIC1, ELECTRIC2\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}DELAY\_SEND\_LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Delay send level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocal FX output level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}OUTPUT\_ASSIGN\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Output routing: EFX1, EFX2, DLY, REV, or DIR\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}PAN\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Stereo pan (L64\textendash{}63R, center 0)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}PROGRAM\_LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Program output level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}PROGRAM\_TEMPO\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Program tempo (5.00\textendash{}300.00 BPM)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}REVERB\_SEND\_LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Reverb send level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCAL\_EFFECT\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocal effect type: OFF, VOCODER, or AUTO\sphinxhyphen{}PITCH\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCAL\_EFFECT\_NUMBER\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocal effect preset (1\textendash{}21). Address unverified in JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi guide.\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCAL\_EFFECT\_PART\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocal effect part: Part 1 or Part 2\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_ENVELOPE\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder envelope: SHARP, SOFT, or LONG\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder output level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_MIC\_HPF\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder mic HPF: BYPASS or 1000\textendash{}16000 Hz\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_MIC\_MIX\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder mic mix level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_MIC\_SENS\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder mic sensitivity (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_SWITCH\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Enable or bypass vocoder\textquotesingle{}, \textquotesingle{}VOCODER\_SYNTH\_LEVEL\textquotesingle{}: \textquotesingle{}Vocoder synth level (0\textendash{}127)\textquotesingle{}\}}}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.effects:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-effects-vocal-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
VocalFXEditor Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{VocalFXEditor} class, a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based editor for controlling
the Vocal FX section of the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer. It provides a graphical interface
for adjusting various vocal effects such as vocoder settings, auto\sphinxhyphen{}pitch parameters,
and mixer controls.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Scrollable UI with multiple tabs for organizing vocal effect settings.
\sphinxhyphen{} Support for vocoder controls, including envelope, mic sensitivity, and synthesis levels.
\sphinxhyphen{} Auto\sphinxhyphen{}pitch settings with selectable pitch type, scale, key, and gender adjustment.
\sphinxhyphen{} Mixer section for controlling levels, panning, reverb, and delay send levels.
\sphinxhyphen{} MIDI integration for real\sphinxhyphen{}time parameter control using \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper}.
\sphinxhyphen{} Dynamic instrument image loading to visually represent the effect in use.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} PySide6 for UI components.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} for sending MIDI messages to the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{VocalFXParameter} for managing effect\sphinxhyphen{}specific MIDI parameters.
\index{VocalFXEditor (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal)@\spxentry{VocalFXEditor}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.effects.vocal}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-helpers-preset-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
preset retrieval

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example:
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} get\_preset\_parameter\_value(parameter=”msb”, id=”001”)
95
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{} get\_preset\_parameter\_value(parameter=”lsb”, id=”010”)
64
\index{preset\_to\_jdxi\_bank\_pc() (in module jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset)@\spxentry{preset\_to\_jdxi\_bank\_pc()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers:jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset.preset_to_jdxi_bank_pc}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.preset.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{preset\_to\_jdxi\_bank\_pc}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{msb}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{lsb}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{pc}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html\#typing.Tuple}{Tuple}\DUrole{p}{{[}}\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{,}\DUrole{w}{ }\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}\DUrole{p}{{]}}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Convert preset (msb, lsb, pc) to JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi bank select + program change format.

\sphinxAtStartPar
The JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi uses LSB 64 for presets 1\sphinxhyphen{}128 and LSB 65 for presets 129\sphinxhyphen{}256.
MIDI Program Change is 0\sphinxhyphen{}127, so presets 129+ must use the second bank.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{msb}} \textendash{} Bank MSB from preset (e.g. 95 for Digital Synth)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{lsb}} \textendash{} Bank LSB from preset (typically 64 in preset data)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{pc}} \textendash{} Preset number 1\sphinxhyphen{}based (1\sphinxhyphen{}256 for Digital Synth)

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Returns}
\sphinxAtStartPar
(msb, lsb, midi\_pc) where midi\_pc is 0\sphinxhyphen{}127

\end{description}\end{quote}

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.helpers:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-helpers-program-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.helpers.program}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDI Program Management and Calculation Utilities

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides utilities for retrieving and calculating
information related to MIDI programs, including the ability to get program details by ID,
calculate MSB (Most Significant Byte), LSB (Least Significant Byte),
and Program Change (PC) values based on bank and program numbers,
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
as well as logging useful program and MIDI data.
\end{quote}
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_program\_index\_by\_id(program\_id: str) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} Optional{[}int{]}:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieves the index of a program by its ID from the \sphinxtitleref{PROGRAM\_LIST}.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_program\_by\_id(program\_id: str) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} Optional{[}Dict{[}str, str{]}{]}:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieves a program by its ID from the \sphinxtitleref{PROGRAM\_LIST}.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{calculate\_midi\_values(bank: str, program\_number: int) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} tuple:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates the MSB, LSB, and PC based on the given bank and program number.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{calculate\_index(bank: str, program\_number: int) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} int:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Calculates the program index based on the bank and program number.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{log\_midi\_info(msb: int, lsb: int, pc: int):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Logs the MSB, LSB, and PC values for MIDI operations.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{log\_program\_info(program\_name: str, program\_id: Optional{[}str{]} = None, program\_details: Optional{[}Dict{]} = None):}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Logs information about the loaded program.

\end{description}

\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{get\_msb\_lsb\_pc(program\_number: int) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} tuple:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Retrieves the MSB, LSB, and PC for a specific program number from the \sphinxtitleref{PROGRAM\_LIST}.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Constants:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PROGRAM\_LIST: A list of dictionaries containing MIDI program information used throughout the functions.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Logging:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module uses Python’s \sphinxtitleref{logging} module to log key operations and errors, such as retrieving programs, calculating MIDI values,
and verifying program values within valid ranges.

\sphinxlineitem{Usage Example:}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{msb}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{lsb}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{pc} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{calculate\PYGZus{}midi\PYGZus{}values}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{A}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{l+m+mi}{5}\PYG{p}{)}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{log\PYGZus{}midi\PYGZus{}info}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{msb}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{lsb}\PYG{p}{,} \PYG{n}{pc}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.pattern:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.pattern:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-pattern-pattern-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.pattern.pattern}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module: Pattern Sequencer with MIDI Integration

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module implements address Pattern Sequencer using PySide6, allowing users to toggle
sequence steps using address grid of buttons. It supports MIDI input to control button states
using note keys (e.g., C4, C\#4, etc.).

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} 4 rows of buttons labeled as Digital Synth 1, Digital Synth 2, Analog Synth, and Drums.
\sphinxhyphen{} MIDI note\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}button mapping for real\sphinxhyphen{}time control.
\sphinxhyphen{} Toggle button states programmatically or via MIDI.
\sphinxhyphen{} Styled buttons with illumination effects.
\sphinxhyphen{} Each button stores an associated MIDI note and its on/off state.
\sphinxhyphen{} Start/Stop playback buttons for sequence control. ..

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.base}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-synth-base-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Synth Control Base Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{SynthControlBase} class, a Qt\sphinxhyphen{}based widget that provides MIDI
control functionality for synthesizer parameters in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi editor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
It facilitates:
\sphinxhyphen{} Sending and receiving MIDI SysEx messages.
\sphinxhyphen{} Handling parameter updates through UI elements (sliders, combo boxes, spin boxes, switches).
\sphinxhyphen{} Managing MIDI helper instances for communication.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} PySide6 for GUI components.
\sphinxhyphen{} jdxi\_editor.midi for MIDI communication.
\sphinxhyphen{} jdxi\_editor.ui.widgets for UI elements.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Classes:
\sphinxhyphen{} SynthControlBase: A base widget for controlling synth parameters via MIDI.
\index{SynthBase (class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base)@\spxentry{SynthBase}\spxextra{class in jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base}}

\index{get\_control\_registry() (jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base.SynthBase method)@\spxentry{get\_control\_registry()}\spxextra{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.base.SynthBase method}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.base.SynthBase.get_control_registry}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{get\_control\_registry}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{synth\_type}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/stdtypes.html\#str}{str}}}\sphinxparamcomma \sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{partial\_no}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{\sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#int}{int}}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{o}{=}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{default_value}{1}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ ControlRegistry}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Return the ControlRegistry singleton instance (shared across all partials).
Note: ControlRegistry is now a singleton, so all partials share the same registry.
\begin{quote}\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Parameters}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxstyleliteralstrong{\sphinxupquote{synth\_type}} \textendash{} JDXiSynth.ANALOG\_SYNTH, DIGITAL\_SYNTH\_1, DIGITAL\_SYNTH\_2, DRUM\_KIT

A mixin for managing partial controls in the digital synth editor.
Provides methods for enabling/disabling partials, updating parameters,
and handling state changes for partials.

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-synth-editor-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
synth\_editor.py

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{SynthEditor} class, a base class for all editor windows in the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Manager application.
It provides an interface for editing synthesizer parameters, handling MIDI messages, and updating UI components.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} UI Elements: Uses PySide6 widgets, including ComboBoxes, Sliders, and SpinBoxes, to adjust synthesizer parameters.
\sphinxhyphen{} MIDI Integration: Sends and receives MIDI messages via \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper}, supporting parameter changes, SysEx communication,
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
and program change handling.
\end{quote}
\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Preset Management: Loads, updates, and applies instrument presets with \sphinxtitleref{PresetHandler} and \sphinxtitleref{PresetLoader}.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Parameter Control: Dynamically creates UI controls for synthesizer parameters, supporting bipolar values and digital conversion.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Shortcuts: Implements keyboard shortcuts for refreshing data and closing the window.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} PySide6 for the UI components.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi} for MIDI communication.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.midi.data.parameter} for synthesizer parameter handling.
\sphinxhyphen{} \sphinxtitleref{jdxi\_manager.ui.style} for applying UI styles.
\index{get\_storage\_scope() (in module jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor)@\spxentry{get\_storage\_scope()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.editor.get_storage_scope}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-synth-partial-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.partial}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Module for the PartialEditor widget, which provides a UI for editing individual partial parameters of a synthesizer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{PartialEditor} class, which extends \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} to offer an interface for modifying
synth parameters through sliders, combo boxes, and spin boxes. It integrates with a MIDI helper to send parameter
changes to the synthesizer in real\sphinxhyphen{}time.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
PartialEditor: A QWidget\sphinxhyphen{}based editor for modifying individual partial parameters.

\sphinxlineitem{Dependencies:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtWidgets (QWidget)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
logging

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
typing (Dict)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager.midi.data.parameter.synth (SynthParameter)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager.midi.data.constants (PART\_1)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager.ui.widgets.slider (Slider)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager.ui.widgets.combo\_box.combo\_box (ComboBox)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_manager.ui.widgets.spin\_box.spin\_box (SpinBox)

\end{itemize}

\end{description}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors.synth:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-synth-simple-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.synth.simple}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Arpeggio Editor Module

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{ArpeggioEditor} class, a specialized editor for configuring arpeggiator
settings within a synthesizer. It extends the \sphinxtitleref{SynthEditor} class, providing a user\sphinxhyphen{}friendly
interface to control various arpeggiator parameters.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Classes:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
ArpeggioEditor: A \sphinxtitleref{QWidget} subclass that allows users to modify arpeggiator parameters
such as style, grid, duration, velocity, accent, swing, octave range, and motif.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Features:}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Provides an intuitive UI with labeled controls and dropdown menus for parameter selection.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Includes a toggle switch to enable or disable the arpeggiator.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Displays an instrument image for better user engagement.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Uses MIDI integration to send real\sphinxhyphen{}time parameter changes to the synthesizer.

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
Supports dynamic visualization and interaction through sliders and combo boxes.

\end{itemize}

\sphinxlineitem{Usage:}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{PySide6}\PYG{n+nn}{.}\PYG{n+nn}{QtWidgets}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{QApplication}
\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{midi\PYGZus{}helper}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{MIDIHelper}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.config module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors:module-jdxi_editor.ui.editors.config}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.editors:jdxi-editor-ui-editors-config-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.config@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.config}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.config@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.editors.config}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Configuration of Editor classes for the JDXI Editor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Stores
\sphinxhyphen{}Title: The title of the editor.
\sphinxhyphen{}Editor Class: The class that implements the editor functionality.
\sphinxhyphen{}Synth Type: Optional type of synthesizer associated with the editor.
\sphinxhyphen{}MIDI Channel: Optional MIDI channel for the editor.
\sphinxhyphen{}icon: Icon for the editor, represented as a string.
\sphinxhyphen{}Keyword Arguments: Additional parameters for the editor.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Example usage:
“arpeggio”: EditorConfig(
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
title=”Arpeggiator”,
editor\_class=ArpeggioEditor,
icon=”ph.music\sphinxhyphen{}notes\sphinxhyphen{}simple\sphinxhyphen{}bold”
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
),

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.instrument module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:module-jdxi_editor.ui.image.instrument}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:jdxi-editor-ui-image-instrument-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.image.instrument@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.instrument}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.instrument@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.instrument}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Module: JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Instrument Display Renderer}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:module-jd-xi-instrument-display-renderer}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides functions to generate a visual representation of the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer
interface using PySide6. It renders key UI elements, including the digital, sequencer section,
and keyboard.


\subparagraph{Functions}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:functions}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:jdxi_editor.ui.image.instrument.draw_instrument_pixmap}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{draw\_instrument\_pixmap()}}}}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{draw\_display()}}

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
{\hyperref[\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:jdxi_editor.ui.image.instrument.draw_sequencer}]{\sphinxcrossref{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{draw\_sequencer()}}}}}

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:dependencies}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtCore (Qt)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtGui (QFont, QPixmap, QImage, QPainter, QPen, QColor)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.dimensions (JDXI\_WIDTH, JDXI\_HEIGHT, etc.)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
These functions generate and digital a graphical representation of the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi’s controls,
which can be integrated into a larger PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based UI.

\subparagraph{Module: base64\_image\_converter}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:module-base64-image-converter}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides a utility function for converting a Base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded image string into a QPixmap.


\subparagraph{Functions:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:id1}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} \begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{base64\_to\_pixmap(base64\_str: str) \sphinxhyphen{}\textgreater{} QPixmap}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Decodes a Base64 string into a QPixmap image.

\end{description}

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Dependencies:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:id2}}\begin{itemize}
\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
base64 (Standard Library)

\item {} 
\sphinxAtStartPar
PySide6.QtGui (QPixmap)

\end{itemize}


\subparagraph{Usage:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:id3}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module is useful for handling image data stored in Base64 format, such as images transmitted over networks
or embedded in JSON responses.


\subparagraph{Example:}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:example}}
\begin{sphinxVerbatim}[commandchars=\\\{\}]
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{k+kn}{from}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{n+nn}{base64\PYGZus{}image\PYGZus{}converter}\PYG{+w}{ }\PYG{k+kn}{import} \PYG{n}{base64\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}pixmap}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{base64\PYGZus{}string} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}\PYG{l+s+s2}{iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAA...}\PYG{l+s+s2}{\PYGZdq{}}
\PYG{g+gp}{\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{}\PYGZgt{} }\PYG{n}{pixmap} \PYG{o}{=} \PYG{n}{base64\PYGZus{}to\PYGZus{}pixmap}\PYG{p}{(}\PYG{n}{base64\PYGZus{}string}\PYG{p}{)}
\end{sphinxVerbatim}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:module-jdxi_editor.ui.image.waveform}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.image:jdxi-editor-ui-image-waveform-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
waveform\_icons

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module provides functions to generate PNG images representing different waveform icons
using the Python Imaging Library (PIL). Each function returns address base64\sphinxhyphen{}encoded string of
the generated image.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
generate\_waveform\_icon(icon\_type, foreground\_color, icon\_scale): Generates address
\sphinxhyphen{} triangle: Generates address triangle waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} upsaw: Generates an upward sawtooth waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} square: Generates address square waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} sine: Generates address sine waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} noise: Generates address noise waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} spsaw: Generates address special sawtooth waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} pcm: Generates address PCM waveform icon.
\sphinxhyphen{} adsr: Generates an ADSR envelope waveform icon.

\end{description}
\index{generate\_waveform\_icon() (in module jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform)@\spxentry{generate\_waveform\_icon()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.ui.image.waveform}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.io:module-jdxi_editor.ui.io.controls}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.io:jdxi-editor-ui-io-controls-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Save all controls to a single JSON file.
This module provides a function to save the controls from multiple editor instances
to a single JSON file. The function takes a list of editor instances and a file path
as input, and it combines the controls into a single JSON object before saving it.
\index{save\_all\_controls\_to\_single\_file() (in module jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls)@\spxentry{save\_all\_controls\_to\_single\_file()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.ui.io.controls}}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-jdxi-instrument-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.instrument}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Create editor instances
analog\_editor = AnalogSynthEditor(midi\_helper, preset\_helper)
digital\_editor = DigitalSynthEditor(midi\_helper, preset\_helper)
drum\_editor = DrumSynthEditor(midi\_helper, preset\_helper)

\sphinxAtStartPar
\# Save all controls to a single JSON file
save\_all\_controls\_to\_single\_file(
\begin{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
editors={[}self.analog\_editor, self.digital\_synth2\_editor self.digital\_synth1\_editor, analog\_editor{]},
file\_path=”all\_controls.json”
\end{quote}

\sphinxAtStartPar
)
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Instrument class for managing presets and MIDI settings.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{JdxiInstrument} class, which extends from the \sphinxtitleref{JdxiUi} class to manage JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi instrument presets, MIDI communication, and UI interactions. It allows for controlling and modifying different preset types (Digital 1, Digital 2, Analog, Drums) and provides MIDI connectivity for program changes and preset management.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Handles MIDI connectivity and communication, including program change signals.
\sphinxhyphen{} Manages different preset types (Digital, Analog, Drums) with the ability to select and load presets.
\sphinxhyphen{} Provides MIDI indicators to digital the status of MIDI input/output ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Includes functionality for dragging the window and selecting different synth types.
\sphinxhyphen{} Integrates with external MIDI devices for seamless performance control.
\sphinxhyphen{} Includes a custom UI to manage and visualize the instrument’s preset settings.
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports the auto\sphinxhyphen{}connection of JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi and provides MIDI configuration if auto\sphinxhyphen{}connection fails.


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~\_\_init\_\_}}}
\pysigstopsignatures

Initializes the instrument’s MIDI settings, UI components, and preset handlers.

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-jdxi-ui-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.ui}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Editor UI setup.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class defines the main user interface for the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Editor application, inheriting from QMainWindow. It handles the creation of the UI elements, including the main layout, menus, status bar, and MIDI indicators. It also provides functionality for displaying and managing synth presets, favorites, and MIDI connectivity.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Sets up a frameless window with a customizable layout.
\sphinxhyphen{} Initializes MIDI helper and indicators for MIDI input/output.
\sphinxhyphen{} Loads and displays a digital font for instrument displays.
\sphinxhyphen{} Allows users to manage and load favorite presets.
\sphinxhyphen{} Displays a virtual piano keyboard in the status bar.
\sphinxhyphen{} Integrates with MIDIHelper for MIDI communication.
\sphinxhyphen{} Loads and saves application settings and preferences.


\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~\_\_init\_\_}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Initializes the UI, settings, MIDI components, and layout.

\end{fulllineitems}



\begin{fulllineitems}

\pysigstartsignatures
\pysigline
{\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{\sphinxhyphen{}~\_create\_main\_layout}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Sets up the central layout for displaying instrument images and overlays.

\end{fulllineitems}

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.jdxi:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-jdxi-utils-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.jdxi.utils}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Utility functions for the JDXI editor UI.

\sphinxAtStartPar
These functions are used to digital messages, handle MIDI communication
and manage MIDI input/output ports.

\subparagraph{config\_dialog module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi:config-dialog-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIConfigDialog is a dialog class that allows users to configure MIDI input and output ports.

\sphinxAtStartPar
It provides the following functionality:
\sphinxhyphen{} Display available MIDI input and output ports in combo boxes.
\sphinxhyphen{} Allow users to select and change MIDI input and output ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Refresh the list of available MIDI ports.
\sphinxhyphen{} Retrieve the selected MIDI port settings.

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-midi-debugger-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{debugger module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi:debugger-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDI Debugger for monitoring and interacting with MIDI commands and SysEx messages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This class provides a graphical user interface (GUI) for sending, decoding,
and logging MIDI messages, including SysEx messages. It allows the user to input
MIDI commands in hexadecimal format, send them to a connected MIDI device,
and view the decoded output for further analysis. The debugger supports both
standard MIDI messages and Roland\sphinxhyphen{}specific address\sphinxhyphen{}based SysEx messages,
including their parameters, values, and checksums.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Key Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Send MIDI messages in hexadecimal format to a MIDI device.
\sphinxhyphen{} Decode Roland SysEx messages with detailed information, including areas, commands, and parameters.
\sphinxhyphen{} Display decoded message data in a readable table format.
\sphinxhyphen{} Log responses from MIDI devices, including message sending success and failure information.
\sphinxhyphen{} Validate checksum for SysEx messages to ensure message integrity.
\sphinxhyphen{} Provides an easy\sphinxhyphen{}to\sphinxhyphen{}use interface with instructions, buttons, and output areas for effective debugging.
\index{SYSEX\_AREAS (in module jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger)@\spxentry{SYSEX\_AREAS}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.midi.debugger}}

\subparagraph{message\_debug module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.midi:message-debug-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
MIDIMessageDebug is a Qt\sphinxhyphen{}based main window for logging and displaying MIDI messages.
It provides a real\sphinxhyphen{}time log view where MIDI messages can be logged with timestamps,
allowing for easy debugging of MIDI communication.

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-patch-manager-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.manager}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Patch Manager Module
====================s

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{PatchManager} class, a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based GUI for loading
and saving MIDI patch files for the Roland JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi synthesizer.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Allows users to browse for patch files using a file dialog.
\sphinxhyphen{} Supports both saving and loading patches, depending on the mode.
\sphinxhyphen{} Integrates with \sphinxtitleref{MIDIHelper} for handling MIDI patch operations.
\sphinxhyphen{} Implements a simple, dark\sphinxhyphen{}themed UI with action buttons.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Classes:
\sphinxhyphen{} PatchManager: A QMainWindow that provides a user interface for managing patch files.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} PySide6
\sphinxhyphen{} jdxi\_editor.midi.io.MIDIHelper
\sphinxhyphen{} jdxi\_editor.ui.style.Style

\subparagraph{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.name\_editor module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:module-jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch.name_editor}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:jdxi-editor-ui-windows-patch-name-editor-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.name\_editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.name\_editor}}\index{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.name\_editor@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.ui.windows.patch.name\_editor}!module@\spxentry{module}}

\subparagraph{Patch Name Editor Module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:patch-name-editor-module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
This module defines the \sphinxtitleref{PatchNameEditor} class, a PySide6\sphinxhyphen{}based dialog for
editing the name of a JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi patch.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Features:
\sphinxhyphen{} Provides a simple UI for renaming patches with a maximum of 12 characters.
\sphinxhyphen{} Ensures patch names are converted to uppercase, matching JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi conventions.
\sphinxhyphen{} Includes “Save” and “Cancel” buttons for user confirmation.
\sphinxhyphen{} Applies a custom dark theme for styling.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Classes:
\sphinxhyphen{} PatchNameEditor: A QDialog that allows users to modify a patch name.

\sphinxAtStartPar
Dependencies:
\sphinxhyphen{} PySide6
\sphinxhyphen{} jdxi\_editor.ui.style.Style


\subparagraph{Example Usage}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor.ui.windows.patch:example-usage}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
\textgreater{}\textgreater{}\textgreater{}dialog = PatchNameEditor(current\_name=”Piano”)
…if dialog.exec\_():  \# If the user clicks Save
…    sys\_ex\_bytes = dialog.get\_sysex\_bytes()
…    print(“SysEx Bytes:”, sys\_ex\_bytes)

\subsection{jdxi\_editor.main module}
\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor:module-jdxi_editor.main}}\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor:jdxi-editor-main-module}}\index{module@\spxentry{module}!jdxi\_editor.main@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.main}}\index{jdxi\_editor.main@\spxentry{jdxi\_editor.main}!module@\spxentry{module}}
\sphinxAtStartPar
Main entry point for the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Editor application.

\sphinxAtStartPar
This module sets up the application environment, including logging, MIDI message listening,
and application window initialization. It also manages the application’s lifecycle,
from initialization to event loop execution.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{Functions:}
\sphinxAtStartPar
midi\_callback(msg): Callback function for handling incoming MIDI messages.

\sphinxAtStartPar
listen\_midi(port\_name, callback): Listens for MIDI messages on the specified port
and triggers the provided callback function.
\begin{description}
\sphinxlineitem{setup\_logging(): Configures logging for the application, including console and file}
\sphinxAtStartPar
logging with rotation.

\end{description}

\sphinxAtStartPar
main(): Main entry point to initialize and run the JD\sphinxhyphen{}Xi Editor application,
set up the window, and handle MIDI message listening.

\end{description}
\index{load\_translations() (in module jdxi\_editor.main)@\spxentry{load\_translations()}\spxextra{in module jdxi\_editor.main}}

\begin{fulllineitems}
\phantomsection\label{\detokenize{jdxi_editor:jdxi_editor.main.load_translations}}
\pysigstartsignatures
\pysiglinewithargsret
{\sphinxcode{\sphinxupquote{jdxi\_editor.main.}}\sphinxbfcode{\sphinxupquote{load\_translations}}}
{\sphinxparam{\DUrole{n}{app}\DUrole{p}{:}\DUrole{w}{ }\DUrole{n}{QApplication}}}
{{ $\rightarrow$ \sphinxhref{https://docs.python.org/3/library/functions.html\#bool}{bool}}}
\pysigstopsignatures
\sphinxAtStartPar
Load Qt .qm translation files for the current locale (e.g. en\_GB for ‘Bar’ instead of ‘Measure’).
Place .qm files in jdxi\_editor/i18n/ or in resources/translations/. Returns True if a translator was installed.

\end{fulllineitems}
